entries.englishNotes: Molotov is an active item in Enter the Gungeon, and a powerup in Exit the Gungeon. The Convict starts with this item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Upon use, throws a Molotov that shatters upon hitting the ground, unleashing a large pool of fire goop that sets enemies on fire and does continuous damage. XTG When collected, all enemies on screen are set on fire. Notes: The Molotov does not function in the Gorguns boss room, as instead of shattering upon hitting the water, it will sink harmlessly below the surface. As the Molotov deals exclusively fire damage, it is useless against enemies that are immune to fire such as Muzzle Wisps or the Bullet King.

entries.englishNotes: Hunter's Journal is an unused passive item. Effects: Increases room drop rate. Trivia: It is unknown why this item was cut. Likely a cut starting item for The Hunter. Probably replaced by the Dog, which has the same effect.
entries.chineseNotes: Hunter's Journal is an unused passive item. Effects: Increases room drop rate. Trivia: It is unknown why this item was cut. Likely a cut starting item for The Hunter. Probably replaced by the Dog, which has the same effect.

entries.englishNotes: El Tigre is an unused gun that fires homing teeth. Notes: This gun behaves in a similar manner to Cat Claw, and also shares a "cat" theme with it. Trivia: It is unknown why this gun was cut. This gun might be a reference to El Tigr3, a musician who was featured in Hotline Miami: Wrong Number OST. It might be a reference to the Community episode Modern Warfare.

entries.englishNotes: Bait Launcher is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Launches steaks. Upon impact, a tiger will appear from off screen and run to the nearest enemy, mauling them before disappearing. Steaks leave a small pool of blobulon goop (likely intended to represent blood) upon landing. Notes: The tiger that appears won't necessarily chase the meat, but rather targets the enemy closest to where it lands, no matter how far the target actually is from the meat. If the player has Snowballets, the tigers' damage output will scale as their size increases. The tigers can get quite large, occupying a majority of the screen.
entries.chineseNotes: 饵枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Launches steaks. Upon impact, a tiger will appear from off screen and run to the nearest enemy, mauling them before disappearing. Steaks leave a small pool of blobulon goop (likely intended to represent blood) upon landing. Notes: The tiger that appears won't necessarily chase the meat, but rather targets the enemy closest to where it lands, no matter how far the target actually is from the meat. If the player has Snowballets, the tigers' damage output will scale as their size increases. The tigers can get quite large, occupying a majority of the screen.

entries.englishNotes: Prototype Railgun is a gun that shoots a high-velocity projectile in a forward line after charging a laser sight beam on target. Notes: Enemies killed by the Prototype Railgun are visibly disintegrated, though this will not stop Shotgun Kin from firing bullet bursts upon their deaths. When this gun is charged, the beam of light coming from the end is usually stopped by walls. However, passages to secret rooms will let the light through, revealing their location. This weapon is close in appearance and function to the Asymmetric Recoilless Carbine-920 railgun of the Halo Franchise.

entries.englishNotes: RC Rocket is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The RC Rocket fires powerful explosive rockets. ETG These rockets behave with the effect of Remote Bullets. On keyboard and mouse controls, these rockets home in towards the players crosshair. Notes: This gun is a reference to the Nikita remote-controlled missile launcher from Metal Gear Solid, which fires controllable missiles.
entries.chineseNotes: RC火箭 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The RC火箭 fires powerful explosive rockets. ETG These rockets behave with the effect of Remote Bullets. On keyboard and mouse controls, these rockets home in towards the players crosshair. Notes: This gun is a reference to the RC火箭 remote-controlled missile launcher from Metal Gear Solid, which fires controllable missiles.

entries.englishNotes: Alpha Bullet is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The first shot of a guns magazine deals 80% more damage. Notes: This item is very effective for guns that have a single bullet in each magazine, as every shot will have increased damage. This item has no effect on beam-style guns. If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases the damage of the first shot of a weapon's clip.

entries.englishNotes: Omega Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The final shot of a guns magazine deals double damage. Notes: This item is very effective for guns that have a single bullet in each magazine, as every shot will have increased damage, as well as guns that have a unique bullet at the end of... This item has no effect on beam guns. The synergies Ruby Weapon, Diamond Weapon, and Emerald Weapon are all bosses from Final Fantasy VII.

entries.englishNotes: Easy Reload Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling reloads a single bullet into the players currently held gun. Notes: This item can be used to repeatedly reload and fire the special last shot of guns like H4mmer. This gun can significantly reduce the effective reload times of single-shot weapons by dodge rolling as soon as fire is pressed.
entries.chineseNotes: 易装子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling reloads a single bullet into the players currently held gun. Notes: This item can be used to repeatedly reload and fire the special last shot of guns like H4mmer. This gun can significantly reduce the effective reload times of single-shot weapons by dodge rolling as soon as fire is pressed.

entries.englishNotes: Brick Breaker is a gun appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The Brick Breaker fires green turtle-shell shaped projectiles which can bounce up to 6 times. ETG Shells can also pierce up to 4 times. XTG In Exit the Gungeon, instead of piercing through enemies, shells will bounce off of enemies, expending one of their six bounces without the need to pierce. Notes: Despite appearing to travel over the ground, Brick Breaker shells are in fact flying, and can travel over pits. This weapon is a reference to the Mario franchise of video games, well known for hiding items in nondescript, breakable bricks. The synergy Block Party is likely a reference to the Mario Party series of Mario games.

entries.englishNotes: Excaliber is a gun that fires a burst of three piercing sword projectiles. Reloading swings the sword, destroying nearby bullets and damaging nearby enemies. Increases curse by 2.5 while held. Notes: Buster Gun - If the player has RPG, Excaliber fires three or four shots in a cone whenever it is reloaded. Snicker-snack - If the player has Vorpal Bullets, Excaliber fires a spread of 3 homing shots that deal 20% less damage. Throw All Daggers - If the player has Knife Shield, it generates two layers of knives upon use. Trivia: Unused: - (CustomSynergyType.SHARDBLADE) If the player has Sprun, it will give Sprun a secondary trigger.

Trivia: Unused: - (CustomSynergyType.SHARDBLADE) If the player has 弹簧支撑, it will give 弹簧支撑 a secondary trigger.

entries.englishNotes: Derringer is a gun. It is a small handgun with only two shots per magazine. If fired after dodge rolling, all bullets can be fired rapidly. Notes: Dead Prez - If the player has the Dueling Pistol, Derringer fires bouncing musket balls identical to the Dueling Pistol, and Dueling Pistol fires a wide spread of 4 bullets alongs... Thorn Bath, ooh! - If the player has Armor of Thorns, Derringer's damage is increased by 30%. This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge you to clear a room with. Trivia: Altered: Like Shooting Fish - This gun used to be able to synergize with Barrel for the synergy Like Shooting Fish.

entries.englishNotes: Shotgun Full of Love is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a spread of 6 disparate bullets with different effects. 2 regular bullets and a candy which have a 25% chance to charm enemies they hit for 10 seconds. A star that can pierce up to 8 times. Notes: Despite firing like a shotgun, the Shotgun Full of Love is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The Shotgun Full of Love being in the CHARM gun class makes it inherently more common than its counterpart, the Shotgun Full of Hate, which exists in the SHOTGUN class.

The 爱之霰弹枪 being in the CHARM gun class makes it inherently more common than its counterpart, the 充满仇恨的霰弹枪, which exists in the SHOTGUN class.

entries.englishNotes: Betrayer's Shield is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon reloading, creates a protective magical shield on the ground. This shield blocks enemy bullets, and will be destroyed upon taking too much damage. All shields in a room are automatically destroyed when the player leaves it. Notes: The Betrayer's Shield is peculiarly a member of the PISTOL gun class despite almost certainly being more at home among the members of the FULLAUTO class. There are a few bullet patterns in certain boss fights that the shield will not block, such as the large bullets that the Cannonbalrog shoots when he disappears and the line of bu...

entries.englishNotes: Triple Crossbow is a gun that fires bolts. Reloading alternates between spreads of three bolts and single bolts that slow enemies. Notes: Triple Sticky - If the player also has Sticky Crossbow, Sticky Crossbow will fire three bolts per shot, and its magazine size will increase from 5 to 6. The Triple Crossbow is in the SHOTGUN gun class and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Trivia: The gun's description is a mathematical inequality stating that three is greater than one, alluding to the fact that the Triple Crossbow's three-shot fire gives it greater offensi...

entries.englishNotes: Sling is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Despite its Ammonomicon description, the Sling is among the most powerful tier weapons- especially against bosses. The Sling dealing additional damage against bosses is a reference to the biblical tale of David and Goliath, where David slays the giant Goliath by the use of his sling. Both the flavor text "Outrageous!" and the Slings and Arrows synergy are references to the famous "To be or not to be" soliloquy in Shakespeare's Hamlet, in which Hamlet refers to...

entries.englishNotes: Flash Ray is a gun that fires piercing beams that have a chance to stun enemies. Notes: Ice Cap - If the player also has Freeze Ray, it freezes enemies much quicker. Savior of the Universe - If the player also has Camera, Flash Ray gains double magazine size, unlimited piercing, a thicker beam, one bounce, increased fire rate, and causes a Cam... Whiter Guon Stone - If the player also has White Guon Stone, the size of the Guon Stone is increased, it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead... Trivia: Everything about this gun is a reference to Flash Gordon. The quote "Ah Ahhh!" is a reference to Queen's song Flash, which is Flash Gordon's theme song.

entries.englishNotes: Phoenix is a gun in Enter the Gungeon Effects: The muzzle flare of this gun is a fiery ejection that sets enemies caught within on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Despite emerging from this fiery belch, the projectile itself does not ignite enemies. Notes: Phoenix's blast of fire does no direct damage, only lighting the enemy on fire. Phoenix Up is likely a reference to the revival item Phoenix Down, from the Final Fantasy series. The shape of the Phoenix is similar to the Shellegun.
entries.chineseNotes: 凤凰 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon Effects: The muzzle flare of this gun is a fiery ejection that sets enemies caught within on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Despite emerging from this fiery belch, the projectile itself does not ignite enemies. Notes: 凤凰's blast of fire does no direct damage, only lighting the enemy on fire. 凤凰 Up is likely a reference to the revival item 凤凰 Down, from the Final Fantasy series. The shape of the 凤凰 is similar to the Shellegun.

entries.englishNotes: Hexagun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires extremely fast, piercing bolts of magic. Has a 20% chance per hit to transmogrify enemies into harmless Chickens. Chickens do not deal contact damage, unless they are jammed. Notes: Bosses are immune to transmogrification. The tagline is a reference to Terry Pratchett's novel The Light Fantastic. Chicken Arise is a reference to the TV Show Aqua Teen Hunger Force.

Chickens do not deal contact damage, unless they are jammed. Notes: Bosses are immune to transmogrification. The tagline is a reference to Terry Pratchett's novel The Light Fantastic. Chicken Arise is a reference to the TV Show Aqua Teen Hunger Force.

entries.englishNotes: Frost Giant is a large gun that fires large bullets. Firing the gun creates a short-ranged cone of ice that can freeze enemies. Notes: The fire rate is only limited by how quickly the player can press the fire button, giving it high potential damage per second. If the player also has Helix Bullets, both bullets follow the same path, as opposed to the normal helix pattern. True to its name, the weapon is so physically large that the Gungeoneers must carry it with both hands, one on the handle and the other under the barrel, despite the gun being a r...

entries.englishNotes: Cobalt Hammer is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires instantaneous piercing blue lasers. When fully charged, the Gungeoneer will strike the gun against the ground, damaging all enemies in a short radius and firing lasers in four to five directions which can electrify... Notes: This gun is a reference to the streamers Cobalt Streak and Richard_Hammer, who before the release of Enter the Gungeon were known for playing The Binding of Isaac, another shooter... The item description refers to their several day long stream leading up to Enter the Gungeon's release, where the two would take turns streaming and sleeping.

entries.englishNotes: Anvillain is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires piercing anvils that knock back and stunETG enemies. Notes: This gun can reliably hit an enemy twice due to high knockback, slow travel speed, and pierce.ETG This guns projectiles are among the few affected by gravity.XTG. The Anvillain is a reference to the Looney Tunes cartoon, Wile E. Coyote and the Road Runner.

entries.englishNotes: Mine Cutter is a gun that upon reloading alternates between firing three small, piercing, parallel lasers and single large lasers. Notes: Dead Place - If the player also has Melted Rock, Mine Cutter's damage is increased by 25%. Mass Cutter - If the player also has Mass Shotgun, when the Mass Shotgun shot splits, 6-10 piercing Mine Cutter lasers will be fired in a cone in the direction the shotgun was fir... If the Mass Shotgun shot hits an enemy before it splits, the lasers will not fire. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the plasma cutter from Dead Space. The alternating fire mode refers to the plasma cutter's rotator module, which allows the cutter to fire either vertically or horizontally.

entries.englishNotes: Staff of Firepower is a gun. Reloading alternates between a rapid fire pistol and a beam-like weapon that can set enemies on fire. Notes: This gun fires faster when shot rapidly like a semi-automatic weapon, rather than holding the trigger like an automatic. Chicken Arise - If the player also has Magic Bullets, the chance for Staff of Firepower to transmogrify enemies and size of bullets are increased. Special Delivery - If the player has Owl, the gun's and Owl's shots bounce, and Owl turns skeletal and fires letters at enemies. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the Fire Rod from the Legend of Zelda series.

entries.englishNotes: Table Tech Sight is a table tech passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Flipping a table briefly slows down time and triples number of bullets fired by the player for three seconds. Notes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, each copy of Table Tech Sight held will multiply the players shots by 3 an additional time. Sometimes flipping tables with duplicates may not activate time slowing down but shots still remain multiplied as usual. "Tabla" is Hindi for "Table", while a sutra is a kind of short Indian literary composition intended to teach rituals, literature, philosophy, law, or a great number of other conce...

Effects: Flipping a table flips all other tables in the room in the same direction that the original table was flipped. Every table flipped has a 40% chance to give 1-4. This also applies to tables flipped indirectly by the first effect of this item. Notes: The base price of Table Tech Money is 50 , despite items normally costing 35. "Tabla" is Hindi for "Table", while a sutra is a kind of short Indian literary composition intended to teach rituals, literature, philosophy, law, or a great number of other conce...

Effects: Flipping a table causes it to fly forward in the direction it was flipped, like a rocket, exploding on impact. This table deals 30 damage to enemies it impacts, and a further 30 damage to enemies caught in its explosion. This explosion can also harm the player, dealing half a heart of damage. Notes: The patch notes for v1.1.3 claimed to have 'fixed' Table Tech Rocket explosions harming the player, however this feature is still present in the game. entries.englishNotes: Gungine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires rapidly. Increases movement speed by 2.25 while held. Reloading while standing in goop clears all goop in a small radius around the player and restores all missing ammo in the current magazine. Notes: Oil Sconces in the Black Powder Mine, Blobulons (and their variants), and goop Barrels in the Oubliette are all extremely useful naturally occurring sources of goop within the Gun... This gun is a reference to the Vlambeer game Luftrausers, which features a bullet-firing engine of the same name. It is also likely a play on 'Gungeon'.
entries.chineseNotes: 喷气式枪 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires rapidly. Increases movement speed by 2.25 while held. Reloading while standing in goop clears all goop in a small radius around the player and restores all missing ammo in the current magazine. Notes: Oil Sconces in the Black Powder Mine, Blobulons (and their variants), and goop Barrels in the Oubliette are all extremely useful naturally occurring sources of goop within the Gun... This gun is a reference to the Vlambeer game Luftrausers, which features a bullet-firing engine of the same name. It is also likely a play on 'Gungeon'.

entries.englishNotes: Snowballer is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires snowballs that slowly freeze enemies. Notes: The snowballs leave behind pools of water, which can be used to put out chest fuses and the fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord. The Frosty the Bullet Tyrant synergy may be a reference to the boss Bleak from Donkey Kong Country 3, a snowman that throws snowballs that appear to increase in size as they trave... Notably, Bleak also shares a similar appearance to Frosty The Snowman.
entries.chineseNotes: 雪球枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires snowballs that slowly freeze enemies. Notes: The snowballs leave behind pools of water, which can be used to put out chest fuses and the fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord. The Frosty the Bullet Tyrant synergy may be a reference to the boss Bleak from Donkey Kong Country 3, a snowman that throws snowballs that appear to increase in size as they trave... Notably, Bleak also shares a similar appearance to Frosty The Snowman.

entries.englishNotes: Melted Rock is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Causes enemy corpses to explode on use, dealing 15 damage to nearby enemies. Notes: This item and its description are references to the character Melting from Nuclear Throne, who is able to cause corpses to explode. The synergy Dead Place is a reference to the game Dead Space, where the first weapon is a repurposed laser used for cutting rock. Bug If the Melted Rock is used after defeating Gatling Gull, when the birds appear to pick up the body, they will all stay in place until the player uses the elevator to the next...

Notes: This item and its description are references to the character Melting from Nuclear Throne, who is able to cause corpses to explode. entries.englishNotes: The Siren is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a narrow spread of 3 water bullets, that burst into pools of water goop upon hitting an obstacle or enemy. Prevents damage from falling into pits while held. Harmful goop has no affect on the player while the Siren is held. This includes status effects like fire or poison, as well as slow effects from Phaser Spider web. Notes: As The Siren creates water, it is able to extinguish the Fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord, and the fuses of fused chests. The Siren is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. A Siren is a mythical creature that lures sailors to their doom, often portrayed as a mermaid.

Prevents damage from falling into pits while held. Harmful goop has no affect on the player while the 塞壬女妖 is held. This includes status effects like fire or poison, as well as slow effects from Phaser Spider web. Notes: As The 塞壬女妖 creates water, it is able to extinguish the Fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord, and the fuses of fused chests. The 塞壬女妖 is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. A 塞壬女妖 is a mythical creature that lures sailors to their doom, often portrayed as a mermaid.

entries.englishNotes: Rattler is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires 7 regular projectiles in a shotgun blast, and an instantaneous green laser that poisons enemies. Bullets from the main shotgun blast travel smoothly, and do not have the slight speed variance seen in other shotguns like the Regular Shotgun. Notes: Despite visually appearing to be and firing like a shotgun, the Rattler is not a part of the SHOTGUN gun class and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The pickup line, "Snakes on A Gun," is a reference to the film Snakes on a Plane. The Twin Snakes synergy is a reference to the Metal Gear Solid series.
entries.chineseNotes: 响尾蛇枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires 7 regular projectiles in a 常规霰弹枪 blast, and an instantaneous green laser that poisons enemies. Bullets from the main 常规霰弹枪 blast travel smoothly, and do not have the slight speed variance seen in other shotguns like the 常规霰弹枪. Notes: Despite visually appearing to be and firing like a 常规霰弹枪, the 响尾蛇枪 is not a part of the SHOTGUN gun class and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The pickup line, "Snakes on A Gun," is a reference to the film Snakes on a Plane. The Twin Snakes synergy is a reference to the Metal Gear Solid series.

entries.englishNotes: Sixth Chamber is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Reduces the cooldown of active items by 5% per point of Curse up to a maximum of 50%. This cooldown reduction stacks with the effects of Coolness, but is still capped to a 50% reduction even when combined. Inverts the negative effect of Curse upon room drops, causing each point of Curse to make room drops more common. Notes: This item can be purchased from Cursula, and doing so is more beneficial than any other means of obtaining the item. It is sufficient to kill just one jammed Trigger Twin in order to unlock the item. Killing minibosses, such as Fuselier, while they're jammed, will also unlock the item.

entries.englishNotes: Busted Television is an active item. It can be found in the elevator shaft of the Gungeon Proper after its shortcut has been unlocked. Effects: Upon use, the television is thrown in the direction of the cursor. This does not deal damage to enemies. Dropped upon dodge rolling. Giving the item to the Blacksmith in the Forge unlocks The Robot. Notes: The television can be stolen from any room by the Resourceful Rat, but only after the player enters the elevator maintenance shaft room for the first time in the run. If the player scares away the Resourceful Rat from stealing the Busted Television, he does not become "scared away" from the room. It can be collected at any point during the Gungeon Proper.

entries.englishNotes: Battery Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. The Robot starts with this item. Effects: Causes water that bullets pass over to become electrified, damaging enemies. Decreases shot spread by 50%, doubling accuracy. Grants immunity to electrified water. Notes: Removed: - If the player has Shock Rifle, shot speed is doubled. Likely removed because it includes a starting item. Cannot be dropped when playing as The Robot. Using items or guns that freeze water such as Frost Bullets and Ice Breaker make this item much less useful, as ice cannot become electrified.

Decreases shot spread by 50%, doubling accuracy. Grants immunity to electrified water. Notes: Removed: - If the player has Shock Rifle, shot speed is doubled. entries.englishNotes: Coolant Leak is an active item. The Robot starts with this item. Effects: Sprays water forward. Notes: Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot. Cannot be manually dropped, but will be forcibly dropped if another active item is obtained. The Sell Creep refuses to buy Coolant Leak if the player manages to place it atop his grate.
entries.chineseNotes: 冷却液泄漏 is an active item. The Robot starts with this item. Effects: Sprays water forward. Notes: Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot. Cannot be manually dropped, but will be forcibly dropped if another active item is obtained. The Sell Creep refuses to buy 冷却液泄漏 if the player manages to place it atop his grate.

entries.englishNotes: Friendship Cookie is an active item. The Cultist starts with this item. Effects: Upon use, resurrects a dead Co-op player with full health. Notes: This item is exclusive to co-op mode and cannot be found via normal gameplay. If cheated into the player's inventory in singleplayer, the Friendship Cookie can be used with no effect.
entries.chineseNotes: 友谊饼干 is an active item. The Cultist starts with this item. Effects: Upon use, resurrects a dead Co-op player with full health. Notes: This item is exclusive to co-op mode and cannot be found via normal gameplay. If cheated into the player's inventory in singleplayer, the 友谊饼干 can be used with no effect.

entries.englishNotes: Hero Sword was an unused gun that was updated in the Farewell to Arms update to an unused active item. Notes: This gun, if spawned through mods or cheats, disappears on being picked up. It likely would have increased Curse by some amount. Trivia: It is unknown why this gun was cut. The Hero Sword reuses an unused sprite for the weapon Blasphemy. The tooltip references the wordplay of Gungeon, but using Sword instead.

entries.englishNotes: Live Ammo is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. The Bullet starts with this item in both games. Effects: Grants contact damage immunity. ETG This does not prevent Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares from setting the player on fire on contact. ETG Increases damage dealt by dodge rolling into enemies by 400% (5x multiplier). Notes: This item is unique to The Bullet and cannot be acquired by other Gungeoneers. Even with this item, Gatling Gulls melee swipe and Wallmongers body still deal contact damage. Electrified Tazies do not deal damage.

Electrified Tazies do not deal damage.

entries.englishNotes: Blasphemy is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, where it functions as the starting weapon of The Bullet. Effects: Fires a piercing rainbow sword projectile when swung. ETG This projectile is only spawned if the player is at full health. The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle. When swung, Blasphemy creates a wide arc that destroys enemy bullets caught within.

The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle. When swung, 渎神 creates a wide arc that destroys enemy bullets caught within.

entries.englishNotes: Bug Boots is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. Notes: Use of this item allows the player to spread poison over the entrance to Bullet Hell, despite there visually being no ground to spread it on.
entries.chineseNotes: 臭虫靴子 is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. entries.englishNotes: Heart Holster is a passive item. Effects: Grants a heart container. Notes: All Out Of Law - If the player has Colt 1851, it fires three tight spreads of three bullets upon reloading. Finger on the Pulse - If the player has Pulse Cannon, upon taking damage, three miniature Pulse cannons briefly spawn around the player and fire at enemies. Quick and the Gundead - If the player has Hip Holster, shots fired by Hip Holster home in on enemies. Trivia: The pickup quote is a reference to the phrase "wear your heart on your sleeve". The "Quick and the Gundead" synergy is a reference to 1995 western film "The Quick and the Dead".

entries.englishNotes: Heart Lunchbox is a passive item. Effects: Grants a heart container. Notes: Finger on the Pulse - If the player also has Pulse Cannon and takes damage, three miniature Pulse Cannons spawn around the player and fire at enemies, before disappearing. Heart-Shaped Box - If the player also has Lament Configurum, it will heal the player instead of dealing damage. Despite being able to be found in and rank chests, it will always have to be bought from a store at rank price. Trivia: The synergy "Heart-Shaped Box" is a reference to the song of the same name by Nirvana.

entries.englishNotes: Heart Locket is a passive item. Effects: Grants a heart container. Notes: Finger on the Pulse - If the player also has Pulse Cannon and takes damage, three miniature Pulse Cannons spawn around the player and fire at enemies, before disappearing. Despite being able to be found in and rank chests, it will always have to be bought from a store at rank price. Like all heart container items, The Robot will receive 5 to 10 instead. Trivia: Unused: Heart Unlocket - (CustomSynergyType.HEART_UNLOCKET) If the player has Shelleton Key, it grants an extra heart container.

entries.englishNotes: Heart Purse is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants a heart container. Notes: Despite being able to be found in and rank chests, it will always have to be bought from a store at rank price. Like all heart container items, The Robot will receive 5 to 10 instead. Like the other heart container items, the Heart Purse will drop alongside a key if it is found from opening an rank chest.

entries.englishNotes: Shotga Cola is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases movement speed by 1.5. Notes: The name and effect of this item are a reference to the caffeinated German chocolate Scho-Ka-Kola, which was initially advertised as an athletic performance enhancer in the 1930s. The Ammonomicon description "save the cap" is a reference to the Fallout franchise, where the caps of beverages such as Nuka-Cola are used as currency. This item may also be a reference to the practice of 'shotgunning' a canned beverage, done by poking a hole in the side of the can to drink through instead of the top.

entries.englishNotes: Shotgun Coffee is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases movement speed by 1.2. Notes: This item's description is a reference to Futurama where Fry drinks 100 cups of coffee, which causes him to move at extreme speeds. "Shotgun" coffee may be a reference to so-called "Bulletproof" coffee, in which mixing unsalted butter into a cup of hot coffee is said to enhance mental clarity.

entries.englishNotes: Liquid Valkyrie is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases enemy bullet speed by 20%. Notes: This item can be counterproductive; since bullets are slower than usual, the time the bullets are in midair is increased, meaning the player can run into bullets that would have a... The name and Ammonomicon quote of this item are references to Max Payne, specifically the performance-enhancing drug Valkyr.

entries.englishNotes: Jar of Bees is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Throws a jar that shatters upon hitting the floor, releasing a swarm of angry bees that seek out and attack nearby enemies. Notes: The thrown jar must impact the ground in order to shatter and release the bees inside. If the jar is tossed into a pit, the jar will not shatter and no bees will be released. The Ammonomicon quote and entry of this item are a reference to the character "The Pain" from Metal Gear Solid 3 who uses bees to attack.

entries.englishNotes: Stuffed Star is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, makes the player rainbow-colored and invincible to most forms of damage. Pits and flame traps can still harm the player. Notes: Despite being immune to the damage of enemy bullets, the player will still be dealt a small amount of knockback when colliding with them. Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Stuffed Star was a item. This item is a reference to the famous invincibility-granting Super Star power-up from Super Mario Bros.

Prior to the Supply Drop Update, 毛绒星星 was a item. This item is a reference to the famous invincibility-granting Super Star power-up from Super Mario Bros.

entries.englishNotes: Bullet Idol is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Taking damage deals 45 damage to all enemies in the room. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Bullet Idol dealt 15 damage instead of 45.
entries.chineseNotes: 子弹偶像 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Taking damage deals 45 damage to all enemies in the room. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, 子弹偶像 dealt 15 damage instead of 45.

entries.englishNotes: Mustache is a passive item. Effects: Grants a ?% chance to heal the player for half a heart upon purchasing an item. Has no effect if the player is playing as The Robot. Makes items in Bello's shop 15% cheaper.XTG Notes: Block Party - If the player has Brick Breaker, the size of its shells is increased by 50%, and their damage is increased by 25%. http://merch.devolverdigital.com - If the player also has Microtransaction Gun, its damage is doubled. Monsters and Monocles - If the player also has Teapot, the AoE effect range of Teapot is increased. Trivia: The mustache item is identical to Bello's mustache, which apparently, he appreciates.

Makes items in Bello's shop 15% cheaper.XTG Notes: Block Party - If the player has Brick Breaker, the size of its shells is increased by 50%, and their damage is increased by 25%. http://merch.devolverdigital.com - If the player also has Microtransaction Gun, its damage is doubled. Monsters and Monocles - If the player also has Teapot, the AoE effect range of Teapot is increased. Trivia: The mustache item is identical to Bello's mustache, which apparently, he appreciates.

entries.englishNotes: Bloodied Scarf is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Replaces the dodge roll with a teleport. Pressing the dodge button teleports the player a short distance in the direction they are moving. Holding down the dodge button allows the player to teleport to the location of the cursor, shown by a shadow of the player's character. Notes: Can be used to blink through locked doors which otherwise require the player to use a key or stand on a pressure plate to open. Does not work if the door is facing downward. Can be used to find secret rooms. The teleport icon will appear grey when over the part of the wall that becomes the path.

Pressing the dodge button teleports the player a short distance in the direction they are moving. Notes: Can be used to blink through locked doors which otherwise require the player to use a key or stand on a pressure plate to open. Does not work if the door is facing downward. Can be used to find secret rooms. The teleport icon will appear grey when over the part of the wall that becomes the path.

entries.englishNotes: Muscle Relaxant is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Reduces bullet spread by 66%, increasing accuracy. Notes: This item is a reference to similar muscle relaxant drugs in the Metal Gear Solid series, such as Diazepam or Pentazemin.
entries.chineseNotes: 肌肉松弛剂 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Reduces bullet spread by 66%, increasing accuracy. Notes: This item is a reference to similar muscle relaxant drugs in the Metal Gear Solid series, such as Diazepam or Pentazemin.

entries.englishNotes: Explosive Decoy is an active item. Effects: Places a decoy that enemies will target. The decoy will explode after taking enough damage or after roughly 11 seconds, dealing 30 damage to nearby enemies. Can be used to steal from shops while placed (until it explodes). Notes: Deadlier Distraction - If the player also has Monster Blood, the Explosive Decoy explodes and creates a large poison pool when attacked. Decoy Octorok - If the player also has Abyssal Tentacle, the decoy will become a charmed enemy. Frostier Distraction - If the player also has Heart of Ice, the Explosive Decoy becomes partially made of ice and causes enemies that shoot it to be frozen.
entries.chineseNotes: 炸弹诱饵 is an active item. Effects: Places a decoy that enemies will target. The decoy will explode after taking enough damage or after roughly 11 seconds, dealing 30 damage to nearby enemies. Can be used to steal from shops while placed (until it explodes). Notes: Deadlier Distraction - If the player also has 怪物血, the 炸弹诱饵 explodes and creates a large poison pool when attacked. 诱饵 Octorok - If the player also has 深渊触手, the decoy will become a charmed enemy. Frostier Distraction - If the player also has 冰之心, the 炸弹诱饵 becomes partially made of ice and causes enemies that shoot it to be frozen.

entries.englishNotes: Bracket Key is an active item. Effects: Deals 150 damage to all enemies in the room. Prevents additional enemy waves from spawning when used. Increases curse by 1 while held. Notes: Hacker (w/ Lower Case r) - Increases damage by 25% while the Lower Case r is held. Square Brace - If the player also has Polaris, it gains 20% increased damage, higher ammo capacity and magazine size, faster fire rate, and a new appearance. The Bracket Key can hit bosses such as Cannonbalrog and High Priest during attacks where they are normally invincible.
entries.chineseNotes: 支架钥匙 is an active item. Effects: Deals 150 damage to all enemies in the room. Prevents additional enemy waves from spawning when used. Increases curse by 1 while held. Notes: Hacker (w/ Lower Case r) - Increases damage by 25% while the Lower Case r is held. Square Brace - If the player also has Polaris, it gains 20% increased damage, higher ammo capacity and magazine size, faster fire rate, and a new appearance. The 支架钥匙 can hit bosses such as Cannonbalrog and High Priest during attacks where they are normally invincible.

entries.englishNotes: Ruby Bracelet is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. If the player has the Ruby Bracelet and talks to the Blacksmith, she will tell the player that this was the first item she made and how she always hated it. Effects: Pre-Upgrade Thrown guns will explode (without destroying them), dealing 30 damage to all nearby enemies. Guns can be thrown by holding the fire button with an empty gun equipped. Notes: Thrown Guns must still be manually re-collected before leaving the room, otherwise they will be stolen by the Resourceful Rat. Only real guns that are actually thrown by the player will count for the Ruby Bracelet.
entries.chineseNotes: 红宝石手链 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. If the player has the 红宝石手链 and talks to the Blacksmith, she will tell the player that this was the first item she made and how she always hated it. Effects: Pre-Upgrade Thrown guns will explode (without destroying them), dealing 30 damage to all nearby enemies. Guns can be thrown by holding the fire button with an empty gun equipped. Notes: Thrown Guns must still be manually re-collected before leaving the room, otherwise they will be stolen by the Resourceful Rat. Only real guns that are actually thrown by the player will count for the 红宝石手链.

entries.englishNotes: Emerald Bracelet is an unused passive item from Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Thrown guns slide back towards the player across the floor for easier collection. Notes: It is unknown why this item was cut. On 28/03/2016, Dodge Roll developer Brent Sodman teased the behaviour of the Emerald Bracelet in a gif posted to twitter with the caption "bonk".

entries.englishNotes: Badge is a passive item in Enter and Exit the Gungeon. It also appears in powerup form in Exit. Notes: ETG While the police officers health is not insignificant, his inability to dodge enemy bullets makes him very vulnerable. Because of this, Badge essentially only gives 20% damage up at the cost of curse, making it worse than even lower-rarity items such as +1 Bullets ETG Dropping the Badge and picking it back up again fully heals the officer.

entries.englishNotes: Big Boy is an active item in Enter the Gungeon Effects: Calls down a missile at the crosshair, causing a large nuclear explosion that deals 150 damage and leaves behind a large pool of poison goop. Enemies within a large radius of the explosion turn into Mutant Bullet Kin. This effect can transform enemies that are normally invulnerable, such as Gunreapers. The explosion will clear enemy bullets in a large area. Notes: The damage done upon use will contribute to its recharge, even without Ice Cube. The Big Boy's explosion transforming enemies into Mutant Bullet Kin is a reference to the mutagenic effects of radiation, such as would contaminate the environment in the wake of...

entries.englishNotes: Trident is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an extremely fast moving ray of light that can pierce once. While reloading, nearby enemy bullets are destroyed, and nearby enemies are pushed away from the player. Notes: Spamming the fire button while dodge rolling causes the gun to fire considerably faster for a brief time after the dodge roll has concluded. This weapon emits light from its tip, illuminating a small area. Gunjurers can catch shots from the Trident, despite them being extremely fast and piercing.
entries.chineseNotes: 三叉戟 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an extremely fast moving ray of light that can pierce once. While reloading, nearby enemy bullets are destroyed, and nearby enemies are pushed away from the player. Notes: Spamming the fire button while dodge rolling causes the gun to fire considerably faster for a brief time after the dodge roll has concluded. This weapon emits light from its tip, illuminating a small area. Gunjurers can catch shots from the 三叉戟, despite them being extremely fast and piercing.

entries.englishNotes: The Scrambler is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires eggs which hatch into 5 homing (Rad: Vel: 270) bullets on impact. Notes: The seeking pellets will always track the enemy closest to the player, not those closest to the point of impact. The seeking pellets from the egg can be caught by Gunjurers, and will retain their homing, making them a formidable threat. This item is a reference to the popular philosophical question - "What came first, the chicken or the egg?"
entries.chineseNotes: 爬行者 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires eggs which hatch into 5 homing (Rad: Vel: 270) bullets on impact. Notes: The seeking pellets will always track the enemy closest to the player, not those closest to the point of impact. The seeking pellets from the egg can be caught by Gunjurers, and will retain their homing, making them a formidable threat. entries.englishNotes: Shield of the Maiden is an active item. Effects: Pulls up a shield that protects the player from bullets and contact damage. The player is immobile while the shield is active. The shield can be active for a maximum of 4 seconds. It will blink right before it's about to go down to alert the player. Notes: If the player has Shield of the Maiden active when swallowed by a Tarnisher, the player will not take damage but will instead lose the item. Certain effects like explosions, poison, fire, and electricity can still damage the player through the shield. It can block the Beholster's eye laser.
entries.chineseNotes: 少女的盾 is an active item. Effects: Pulls up a shield that protects the player from bullets and contact damage. The player is immobile while the shield is active. The shield can be active for a maximum of 4 seconds. It will blink right before it's about to go down to alert the player. entries.englishNotes: Boomerang is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Throws a boomerang that travels around the room and attempts to Stun (Dur: 5s) all enemies. Notes: The Boomerang can locate and target Wall Mimics before they reveal themselves, making it an ideal tool for finding them out. This item is a reference to the Magical Boomerang from The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past. The blue and red on the Boomerang and how the sprite is positioned are similar to the color scheme and position on the regular and magical boomerangs from A Link to the Past.
entries.chineseNotes: 回飞镖 is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Throws a boomerang that travels around the room and attempts to Stun (Dur: 5s) all enemies. Notes: The 回飞镖 can locate and target Wall Mimics before they reveal themselves, making it an ideal tool for finding them out. This item is a reference to the Magical 回飞镖 from The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past. The blue and red on the 回飞镖 and how the sprite is positioned are similar to the color scheme and position on the regular and magical boomerangs from A Link to the Past.

entries.englishNotes: Teleporter Prototype is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Teleports the player somewhere on use. The outcome of each teleport is determined by a controlled list of weighted destination types; Using the Teleporter Prototype in a room with enemies will despawn all active enemies and reset the escaped room, similarly to the Escape Rope. When teleporting into a room containing active enemies, one random enemy in the room will be telefragged in a fountain of blood, dealing 100000 damage. Notes: This item is based upon the static Teleporters used to traverse the chambers of the Gungeon.
entries.chineseNotes: 传送原型机 is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Teleports the player somewhere on use. The outcome of each teleport is determined by a controlled list of weighted destination types; Using the 传送原型机 in a room with enemies will despawn all active enemies and reset the escaped room, similarly to the Escape Rope. When teleporting into a room containing active enemies, one random enemy in the room will be telefragged in a fountain of blood, dealing 100000 damage. Notes: This item is based upon the static Teleporters used to traverse the chambers of the Gungeon.

entries.englishNotes: Armor Synthesizer is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a 10% chance to gain armor upon completing a room without taking damage. The armor will spawn on top of the player.
entries.chineseNotes: 护甲合成器 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a 10% chance to gain armor upon completing a room without taking damage. The armor will spawn on top of the player.

entries.englishNotes: Pig is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a pig companion who mostly does nothing but follow the player around. If the player dies, the pig will jump in the way of the finishing shot, sparing the player and effectively granting an extra life. In Exit the Gungeon, the Pig only heals a small amount of health. Notes: Does not revive a player in coop mode if they are not the last player standing, and may softlock the game. If the player has Baby Good Mimic, acquiring Pig creates a second identical Pig companion. Both companions will sacrifice themselves to save the player. If the player has Clone, Pig dies before Clone can trigger.
entries.chineseNotes: 英雄猪 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a pig companion who mostly does nothing but follow the player around. If the player dies, the pig will jump in the way of the finishing shot, sparing the player and effectively granting an extra life. In Exit the Gungeon, the 英雄猪 only heals a small amount of health. Notes: Does not revive a player in coop mode if they are not the last player standing, and may softlock the game. If the player has 拟身怪乖宝宝, acquiring 英雄猪 creates a second identical 英雄猪 companion. Both companions will sacrifice themselves to save the player. If the player has 克隆人, 英雄猪 dies before 克隆人 can trigger.

entries.englishNotes: Sponge is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Removes all goops in a radius around the player. Allows the player to understand Professor Goopton's dialogue. The player does not need to be actively holding the sponge in order to understand Professor Goopton - they just need to have picked it up once in the run. Notes: Sponge cannot be sold. Godliness is a reference to the idiom, Cleanliness is next to Godliness. The Spengbab synergy is a reference to SpongeBob SquarePants, particularly internet memes revolving around intentional egregious misspelling of the character's names, such as 'spu...

entries.englishNotes: Gas Mask is a passive item. Effects: Grants immunity to poison. Notes: Super Serum - If the player also has Plague Pistol or the Plunger, the guns' ammo capacity is increased by 50%, their projectiles can bounce, and the size of the projectiles incre...

entries.englishNotes: Hazmat Suit is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants immunity to fire, poison, and electricity. Notes: Hazmat Suit grants immunity to the Shockwave modifier's electricity links in Challenge Mode.
entries.chineseNotes: 防护服 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants immunity to fire, poison, and electricity. Notes: 防护服 grants immunity to the Shockwave modifier's electricity links in Challenge Mode.

entries.englishNotes: Ring of Triggers is a passive item. Effects: While using an active item, rapidly fires the currently held weapon in a counterclockwise ring. The effect has a cooldown of about 10 seconds. Notes: While not a synergy in the traditional sense, active items with no cooldown such as Sense of Direction, Jetpack, or IBomb Companion App are strongly recommended to maximize usage... The player can also maximize the benefit of this item by using a gun or bullet modifier that allows the shots fired to bounce or be retargeted, such as Serious Cannon or Remote Bu... The shopkeeper does not count the ring's effect as firing in his shop, making it helpful for finding breakable walls in the shop.
entries.chineseNotes: 扳机之戒 is a passive item. Effects: While using an active item, rapidly fires the currently held weapon in a counterclockwise ring. The effect has a cooldown of about 10 seconds. Notes: While not a synergy in the traditional sense, active items with no cooldown such as 方向指针, 喷气包, or iBomb伙伴应用程序 are strongly recommended to maximize usage... The player can also maximize the benefit of this item by using a gun or bullet modifier that allows the shots fired to bounce or be retargeted, such as 强力加农炮 or Remote Bu... The shopkeeper does not count the ring's effect as firing in his shop, making it helpful for finding breakable walls in the shop.

entries.englishNotes: Armor of Thorns is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage dealt by dodge rolling into enemies by 600% (7x multiplier). For most characters this increases damage dealt per roll from 3 to 21. As The Robot stats with innately higher dodge roll damage, Armor of Thorns instead increases damage dealt per roll from 4 to 28. Notes: Even with this item, Gatling Gulls melee swipe and Wallmongers body still deal contact damage. Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, Armor of Thorns multiplied dodge roll damage by 16. Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this item did not prevent contact damage, making it difficult to use without getting hurt.
entries.chineseNotes: 荆棘之甲 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage dealt by dodge rolling into enemies by 600% (7x multiplier). For most characters this increases damage dealt per roll from 3 to 21. As The Robot stats with innately higher dodge roll damage, 荆棘之甲 instead increases damage dealt per roll from 4 to 28. Notes: Even with this item, Gatling Gulls melee swipe and Wallmongers body still deal contact damage. Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, 荆棘之甲 multiplied dodge roll damage by 16. Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this item did not prevent contact damage, making it difficult to use without getting hurt.

entries.englishNotes: Ring of Ethereal Form is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, the player is invulnerable to damage, can walk through enemies and obstacles, and is capable of flight, but cannot dodge roll. The player can steal from merchants while in ethereal form. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Get Ethereal!" is a play on the phrase 'get real'. It may also be a reference to the chorus line "Get Physical", from the Olivia Newton-John song Physical.
entries.chineseNotes: 以太之戒 is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, the player is invulnerable to damage, can walk through enemies and obstacles, and is capable of flight, but cannot dodge roll. The player can steal from merchants while in ethereal form. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Get Ethereal!" is a play on the phrase 'get real'. It may also be a reference to the chorus line "Get Physical", from the Olivia Newton-John song Physical.

entries.englishNotes: Chaff Grenade is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Stuns enemies in the room for 10 seconds. Can be used to steal items from merchant npcs and the Shop if used in the same room. Has a limited number of uses, and disappears after being used 3 times. Notes: Chaff Grenade can be found multiple times in a single run, and will grant 3 uses each time it is picked up. It can still be sold for its full sell price at 2 or 1 uses left. This item is a reference to a weapon of the same name often found in the Metal Gear Solid series of games.
entries.chineseNotes: 干草手榴弹 is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Stuns enemies in the room for 10 seconds. Can be used to steal items from merchant npcs and the Shop if used in the same room. Has a limited number of uses, and disappears after being used 3 times. Notes: 干草手榴弹 can be found multiple times in a single run, and will grant 3 uses each time it is picked up. It can still be sold for its full sell price at 2 or 1 uses left. This item is a reference to a weapon of the same name often found in the Metal Gear Solid series of games.

entries.englishNotes: Blank Companion's Ring is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants a living blank companion who activates a Blank effect each time an active item is used. Has a cooldown of 10 seconds between each activation. Notes: The blank effect activates in the room the companion is currently in, not necessarily the one the player is in. This is important to note when using the item to open secret rooms.
entries.chineseNotes: Blank Companion's Ring is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants a living blank companion who activates a Blank effect each time an active item is used. Has a cooldown of 10 seconds between each activation. Notes: The blank effect activates in the room the companion is currently in, not necessarily the one the player is in. This is important to note when using the item to open secret rooms.

entries.englishNotes: Smoke Bomb is an active item. Effects: Temporarily Stealths the player, rendering them invisible to enemies. Can be used to steal items from shops. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Smoke Bomb was a item. Any action, besides walking and stealing, will remove the invisibility. Followers like Wingman will not shoot while the invisibility effect is active.
entries.chineseNotes: 烟雾弹 is an active item. Effects: Temporarily Stealths the player, rendering them invisible to enemies. Can be used to steal items from shops. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, 烟雾弹 was a item. Any action, besides walking and stealing, will remove the invisibility. Followers like Wingman will not shoot while the invisibility effect is active.

entries.englishNotes: Ring of the Resourceful Rat is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: If the Resourceful Rat steals an item or gun, he will drop another item or gun of the same type and quality. The Rat can be traded with once per chamber after acquiring the ring. Unused trades will carry over to subsequent floors, allowing multiple trades on a single chamber. Active items are always exchanged for other active items. Passive items are always exchanged for other passive items. Notes: The Ring of the Resourceful Rat will not be stolen by the Resourceful Rat. The Resourceful Rat will not trade with the player while in the Resourceful Rat's Lair.
entries.chineseNotes: 机智大鼠之戒 is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: If the Resourceful Rat steals an item or gun, he will drop another item or gun of the same type and quality. The Rat can be traded with once per chamber after acquiring the ring. Unused trades will carry over to subsequent floors, allowing multiple trades on a single chamber. Active items are always exchanged for other active items. Passive items are always exchanged for other passive items. Notes: The 机智大鼠之戒 will not be stolen by the Resourceful Rat. The Resourceful Rat will not trade with the player while in the Resourceful Rat's Lair.

entries.englishNotes: Shellegun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Alternates between a pistol form and a continuous beam form when reloaded. Increases Curse by 1 while in the inventory. Notes: The gun's sound effects are identical to the Shelleton's sounds. The entry for the Shellegun in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Shelleton.
entries.chineseNotes: 贝壳枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Alternates between a pistol form and a continuous beam form when reloaded. Increases Curse by 1 while in the inventory. Notes: The gun's sound effects are identical to the Shelleton's sounds. The entry for the 贝壳枪 in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Shelleton.

entries.englishNotes: Table Tech Stun is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Flipping a Table briefly stuns nearby enemies. Notes: "Tabla" is Hindi for "Table", while a sutra is a kind of short Indian literary composition intended to teach rituals, literature, philosophy, law, or a great number of other conce...

Notes: "Tabla" is Hindi for "Table", while a sutra is a kind of short Indian literary composition intended to teach rituals, literature, philosophy, law, or a great number of other conce...

entries.englishNotes: Green Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. ETG Adds a 20% chance to heal upon taking damage. This chance increases to 50% if the damage would have killed the player. Notes: The Green Guon Stone is incapable of healing The Robot. Prior to the A Farewell to Arms Update, Green Guon Stone was a item.
entries.chineseNotes: 绿色滚石 is a passive item and a Guon Stone that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. ETG Adds a 20% chance to heal upon taking damage. This chance increases to 50% if the damage would have killed the player. Notes: The 绿色滚石 is incapable of healing The Robot. Prior to the A Farewell to Arms Update, 绿色滚石 was a item.

entries.englishNotes: Master Round is a passive item. There are five variants of the Master Round, each of which is acquired by defeating the corresponding floor's boss without taking damage. Effects: Grants a heart container. Notes: Master Rounds are one of the necessary materials to unlock shortcuts. Master Rounds will not appear if the player takes any kind of damage during a boss fight, including falling into pits and other kinds of damage not inflicted by the boss itself. Master Rounds will also not appear if the player takes damage during a boss fight, and then restarts the boss fight using the Escape Rope or Teleporter Prototype.

entries.englishNotes: Klobbe is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It rapidly fires inaccurate, low-damage bullets. Notes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with. This gun is a reference to the Klobb from GoldenEye 007 (itself based on the real-life Škorpion), a notoriously bad gun with similar weaknesses as the Klobbe. The Klobbering Time synergy is a reference to Fantastic Four member The Thing, whose catchphrase is "It's clobbering time".

The Klobbering Time synergy is a reference to Fantastic Four member The Thing, whose catchphrase is "It's clobbering time".

entries.englishNotes: Hidden Compartment is a passive item. The Pilot starts with this item. Effects: Grants an extra active item slot. Grants 10% increased maximum ammo for all weapons. entries.englishNotes: Abyssal Tentacle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a tentacle which homes in on and grips enemies, ignoring obstacles. Notes: The tentacle can grip enemies through walls. The tentacle can damage disappearing bosses like Cannonbalrog and High Priest if they are connected and kept while vanished. This gun is a reference to the character "Purple Tentacle" — a sentient, disembodied tentacle with plans for world domination — from the LucasArts game Day of the Tentacle.

entries.englishNotes: Quad Laser is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a large, slow moving laser bullet. Notes: This gun is a reference to the gun used by the Mooninites in the show Aqua Teen Hunger Force. Jumping... Is Useless quotes a scene in the show where the Mooninites use the Quad Laser in an underwhelming battle against a character who retaliates with laser eyes. When Ring of Triggers is used with Jumping... is useless, it will only create 5 Quad Laser shots.

entries.englishNotes: Microtransaction Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Uses instead of ammo. Fires a variety of objects such as gems, books, money, and gungeoneer figurines. Different objects have different effects and damage values; Notes: The expression "Pay To Win" is derived from micro-transaction-heavy games, where an advantage over other players can be achieved by investing more money in the game. The green crystals this gun fires and uses as its ammo counter may be a reference to "Dungeon Keeper", an iOS game by EA which famously blocked player progress unless they separat... The randomness of its projectiles may be a reference to lootboxes, a common in-game purchasable that provides random items to buyers.

entries.englishNotes: Origuni is a gun that fires piercing paper planes that have the Remote Bullets effect and follow the player's crosshair. Notes: Blade Runner - If the player also has Deck4rd, magazine size of both guns is increased by 50%. Paperwork - If the player also has the Ballot or any Table Tech item except for Heat or Shotgun (Blanks, Money, Rage, Rocket, Sight, or Stun), the first shot after reloading will... Paper Lanterns - If the player has Balloon Gun, while holding either gun in a room with enemies, paper lanterns periodically spawn, which drift towards enemies and deal damage. Trivia: The name of the gun comes from origami, the Japanese art of paper-folding.

entries.englishNotes: Banana is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Flings a banana that bounces once, and explodes upon destruction dealing 30 damage to nearby enemies. Each time this banana hits a wall, regardless of whether it bounces or explodes, it creates two more bananas. The banana will also release two sub-bananas if it reaches the end of its range and detonates mid-air, or if it impacts an enemy. Notes: This gun likely is a reference to the game Gorillas, itself referenced with a weapon of the same name from Worms. The pickup quote is a reference to the plantain, a banana cultivar subgroup.

entries.englishNotes: Super Meat Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires piercing, bouncing sawblades. Increases movement speed by 1.5 while held (base movement speed is 7). Grants an empty heart container. Notes: This gun is a reference to the game Super Meat Boy. The sawblades fired by Super Meat Gun are a reference to the abundance of sawblade traps in every area of Super Meat Boy. The Ammonomicon quote "Very Fast" and the first line of the description "Made of Meat" are references to the character Meat Boy's description in the character select menu "Very fa...

entries.englishNotes: Makeshift Cannon is a gun that fires a blast of 6 colored balls that deal extremely high damage. Notes: The Return - If the player also has Pig, the Pig turns green to resemble Cannon, and shoots bat projectiles at nearby enemies. The Makeshift Cannon is one of the few guns able to ignore the Boss DPS cap. Because it only has one shot, it is recommended to only use it during boss fights. When the shots hit enemies and get destroyed they make an explosion, but it takes no damage. Trivia: The weapon's appearance and function is a reference to the Star Trek episode Arena, in which Kirk makes such a weapon out of bamboo, hand-mixed black powder, diamonds, and vines.

entries.englishNotes: Camera is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a charged shot that deals 10 damage to all enemies in the current room. Notes: Shooting the Camera will not activate mimics, damage chests, reveal secret walls, or break curse pots. The Camera can be used to attack bosses during attacks where they are invincible like Cannonbalrog and the High Priest Bug Pausing and unpausing the game after firing Camera on a certain frame will freeze the game on a black screen.

entries.englishNotes: Gunzheng is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing (Rad: 3 Vel: 350) arrows in a very wide spread. Each arrow can pierce up to 3 enemies. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, the arrows did not home in on enemies. If all arrows land on enemies, this gun deals very high damage per second. This is a reference to the Guzheng, a Chinese traditional plucked musical string instrument.

This is a reference to the 枪筝, a Chinese traditional plucked musical string instrument.

entries.englishNotes: Tetrominator is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires random tetromino-shaped bullets that stick to enemies. If a line-piece tetromino hits an enemy, it will explode. This explosion deals more damage the more tetrominos are stuck to that enemy. Notes: This gun is a reference to Tetris. The description is a reference to jokes about the line piece not appearing when needed. The sprite is made of Tetrominoes assembled in a gun shape.

entries.englishNotes: Not to be confused with the Evolver. Devolver is a gun. Its bullets have a high chance to devolve enemies into Arrowkin. Notes: Contractual Obligation - If the player has Gungine, a pool of oil spawns underneath enemies killed using it. Cool Kids Club - If the player has Strafe Gun, continuously moving with the Strafe Gun held gives the player a yellow glow, increasing movement speed. Overdose - If the player also has Heavy Bullets, Heavy Bullets no longer decreases shot speed (this applies for all guns, not just Devolver). Trivia: This gun is a reference to the publisher of Enter the Gungeon, Devolver Digital. Its ammo counter is represented by Ds similar to the Devolver Digital logo.

entries.englishNotes: Orange is a single-use active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases Coolness by 2 while held. Has the following effects on use; Fully heals the player, and their co-op partner if one is present. (No effect for the Robot) Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Orange was a item. This item is a reference to the PS2 video game God Hand. The appearance and function of the item is a reference to one of the possible health pickups in God Hand, an Orange.

entries.englishNotes: Book of Chest Anatomy is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Improves drops from broken chests. 45% chance to drop a random pickup. 50% chance to drop an item or gun based on the chests regular break loot table. (See Chests#Chest Destruction for more information) Notes: Has no impact on the likelihood of finding Gold Junk. Prior to the A Farewell to Arms Update, Book of Chest Anatomy was a item. Can cause Lies (obtainable from destroying the Truth Chest) to be replaced with a Pickup

entries.englishNotes: Ring of Chest Vampirism is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Breaking unopened chests and killing Mimics heals the player for. Notes: Breaking chests has no effect on the health of The Robot. Prior to the A Farewell to Arms Update, Ring of Chest Vampirism was a item.

entries.englishNotes: Gun Soul is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a heart container. Upon death, the player respawns at the start of the current floor with one heart container (or 2 as The Robot), retaining all of their items and guns. All removed heart containers will be returned upon the player reclaiming their soul, however they will be empty. Notes: There is no limit to the number of times that the player can resurrect themselves with Gun Soul, provided they are able to return to their soul and reclaim it without perishing. Bug Using the Cryo Button to save and quit a run while in this spirit form will cause the player to be returned to normal upon re-entering the run.

entries.englishNotes: Brick of Cash is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Causes a living 'Snitch Brick' to appear outside the entrances to Secret rooms, giving away their location. Does not reveal Secret Rooms which cannot be opened with a Blank, such as the Trapdoor Room. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Brick of Cash was an item. When bought, Brick of Cash will not reveal a secret room next to the shop room until the shop is reentered. The Cash Rules Everygun Around Me Synergy is a reference to Wu-Tang Clan's "C.R.E.A.M" quote "Cash rules everything around me".

entries.englishNotes: Wingman is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Orbits the player, firing homing 20-damage rockets at enemies every 5 seconds. These missiles will ignite any flammable goop they pass over. Blocks bullets. Notes: The missiles fired by the Wingman will target a single enemy, and will seek it out until it impacts or the target dies. The Wingman is a miniature version of Z's ship from The Pilot's Past. The name 'Wingman' can refer either to a pilot who flies in the same formation (or 'wing') as another pilot, or more informally to someone who accompanies a friend to offer suppor...

entries.englishNotes: Wolf is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a wolf companion who chases and bites enemies, dealing 5 damage per bite. XTG Wolf can pick up and Powerups, but will not actively seek them out. Notes: ETG Wolf will not cross pits to attack. The Wolf interacts unusually with certain bosses; The Wolf can damage High Priest and Cannonbalrog during their invulnerable attacks.

entries.englishNotes: Briefcase of Cash is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants 250 and 3 when first picked up. Dropping the Briefcase of Cash does not revoke the granted or , meaning it can be safely sold once picked up for an additional 41. Notes: Purchasing the Briefcase of Cash in a shop will result in a net gain of. The Cash Rules Everygun Around Me Synergy is a reference to Wu-Tang Clan's "C.R.E.A.M" quote "Cash rules everything around me". Bug Saving and quitting a run with the Save Button will cause the Briefcase of Cash to give an additional 3 upon resuming the run. This can be done multiple times per run.

Halves shot spread, increasing accuracy. Notes: Will carry over to a Gungeoneer's past. The exception to this is in the Pilot's past, where the item doesn't appear within the player's inventory, but will appear in the ending screen. entries.englishNotes: Unity is a passive item. Effects: Increases damage based on the other guns the player has. 2% of the total damage of the player's non-equipped guns is added as flat damage to the player's currently equipped gun. Notes: Captain Plant It - If the player has Portable Turret, Portable Turret gains Unity's damage bonus. Garbage Collecting - If the player has Trashcannon, hitting an enemy with it causes it and its bullets to briefly freeze. what engine do you use - If the player has Gungine, it fires a spread of three bullets per shot, but fires at 1/5th of its original rate of fire. Trivia: "Our Powers Combined" and the ring design is a reference to Captain Planet. The Captain Plant It synergy also suggests this.

entries.englishNotes: Yari Launcher is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires tightly homing (Rad: Vel: 220) rockets that create small explosions upon hitting an enemy. The damage dealt by these rockets (both their impact damage and explosive damage) bypass the Boss DPS cap, allowing this gun to kill bosses extremely quickly. Notes: The gun is a reference to the rocket launcher in the 2013 reboot of Shadow Warrior. The orbiting Demon Head gained through the Shadow Warrior synergy is likely a reference to one of the game's weapons, a demon head that shoots a laser out of its mouth.

entries.englishNotes: Elder Blank is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, activates a blank. Increases Curse by 2 while held. Notes: Elder Blank does not activate the Blank Shrine. Elder Blank and Daruma are the only active items that can be used while dodge rolling. The Blank Generation synergy may be a reference to the punk album Blank Generation by Richard Hell and the Voidoids.

entries.englishNotes: Hip Holster is a passive item. Effects: Reloading a weapon fires a single bullet from it for free. The bullet fired is always perfectly accurate, even if the gun is not. Notes: This item works well with single shot weapons, effectively doubling their firepower. This item appears to fire the "Default" version of a projectile - for example, on Charged weapons that have an "uncharged" state like the Megahand and Heroine, the Hip Holster wil... Weapons that fire multiple bullets every shot will instead fire only a single bullet, often rendering the projectile near-ineffective. Trivia: The synergy "Pretty Good" is a reference to Revolver Ocelot's quote, "You're pretty good" in Metal Gear Solid.

entries.chineseNotes: Internal-use Pilot ship item. This special active object is not a normal player-facing pickup and no standalone public wiki article was found.

entries.englishNotes: Bullet is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon which fires rotating guns which fire streams of bullets. Notes: Blammo! - If the player also has Shell, the guns are dual-wielded. This gun seems to be in reference to two colloquial sayings that mean relatively the same thing - "Firing a gun" and "Firing a bullet". The smaller bullets fired by launched guns are not affected by the player's Damage stat.

entries.englishNotes: Hyper Light Blaster is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires pink lasers, with high damage per shot. Has an extremely low maximum ammo, however landing a shot on an enemy will restore 1 ammo to the gun. Notes: Shot creatures do not need to be hostile to restore ammo to this gun. As such, shooting chickens and rats will still restore ammunition. Shooting an enemy that is in the midst of its death animation will still restore ammo, as long as the enemy hitbox still blocks bullets. Other shootable targets like chests, cursed pots, or explosive barrels will not restore ammo to the Hyper Light Blaster.

entries.englishNotes: Huntsman is a gun that fires shotgun blasts of 6 bullets. Reloading swings the axe, destroying bullets and knocking back enemies in a short range. Curse is increased by 1 while this gun is held. Notes: Peculiarly, the Huntsman is a part of the FULLAUTO gun class despite not being remotely fully automatic. Because the Huntsman is not SHOTGUN type despite being a shotgun, it does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Trivia: The fact that the weapon increases Curse while being carried stems from the Gungeon rejecting non-bullet based weapons. Executioners wield this weapon. The Huntsman appears to be a Regular Shotgun with an axe blade attached to the underside of the barrel.

entries.englishNotes: Really Special Lute is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a spread of 7 musical note bullets that each have an 8.5% chance to charm enemies for 6 seconds. While firing, buffs the players companions- doubling their damage. This companion buff does not apply to most melee attacks, such as those of the Wolf or Ser Junkan. Notes: This gun is completely unaffected by accuracy increasing items, such as Muscle Relaxant. This gun’s description is a reference to Patrick Rothfuss’s Kingkiller Chronicle book series. The Three Part Harmony synergy may be a reference to the Kingkiller Chronicle, as the main character at one point performs once a difficult ballad requiring them to play three mel...

entries.englishNotes: The Starpew is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to fire droplets of water. This gun has multiple levels of charge, and each level increases the number of droplets fired. No Charge - Fires a single droplet. 0.25 Second Charge - Fires 3 droplets in a line. Notes: This gun is a reference to the Watering Can tool in the game Stardew Valley, a tile-based agricultural game. Originally, Starpew's magazine size was 100 and it did not need to reload.
