Notes: This gun is a reference to the Watering Can tool in the game Stardew Valley, a tile-based agricultural game. Originally, 星露喷水壶's magazine size was 100 and it did not need to reload.

entries.englishNotes: Dueling Laser is a gun that fires a large, high-damage piercing laser. Like an active item, it must recharge after each shot by dealing damage to enemies. Notes: Banshee Cry - If the player has Screecher, it fires three sound waves per shot instead of one. Death Square - If the player has Dueling Pistol, damaging enemies with the Dueling Pistol rapidly recharges the Dueling Laser. During a blessed run, the Dueling Laser does not recharge like an active item. Instead, it automatically reloads in around 7 seconds. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the Dueling Laser Pistol from the book Dying of the Light, the first novel of George R. R. Martin.
entries.chineseNotes: 决斗激光 is a gun that fires a large, high-damage piercing laser. Like an active item, it must recharge after each shot by dealing damage to enemies. Notes: Banshee Cry - If the player has 声波枪, it fires three sound waves per shot instead of one. Death Square - If the player has 决斗手枪, damaging enemies with the 决斗手枪 rapidly recharges the 决斗激光. During a blessed run, the 决斗激光 does not recharge like an active item. Instead, it automatically reloads in around 7 seconds. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the 决斗激光 Pistol from the book Dying of the Light, the first novel of George R. R. Martin.

entries.englishNotes: JK-47 is a gun that was added in the A Farewell to Arms Update. It looks identical to the AK-47 before being picked up, after which its barrel will droop and its true nature will be revealed. Notes: The Killing Joke - If the player also has AK-47, the two guns are dual-wielded. Soft Air - If the player also has Sponge, when holding the JK-47, the player regains ammo when walking over liquids, and increases fear range while reloading. If the player has both synergies at once, AK-47 will also regain ammo when walking over liquids if it is the selected gun of the dual-wield. Trivia: Its name is a portmanteau of AK-47 and JK (i.e. "Just Kidding," referencing the gun's fakeness).

entries.englishNotes: 3rd Party Controller is a gun. Upon hitting an enemy, they will move in the same direction the player moves, and shoot towards the crosshair if the player shoots. Notes: Mad Cats - If the player also has Cat Bullet King Throne, the bullets shot from Cat Bullet King Throne home in on enemies while 3rd Party Controller is equipped. Controlled enemies do not deal contact damage to the player, and can be killed safely by rolling over them. Standing close to enemies also deals a small amount of constant damage to them while controlled. Trivia: The Mad Cats synergy is a reference to MadCatz Interactive, formerly one of the leading producers of third-party gaming controllers.

entries.englishNotes: Shell is a Gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon which fires a spread of 3 shotguns, each of which fires 2 bullets upon hitting an enemy or obstacle. Notes: This gun seems to be in reference to two colloquial sayings that mean relatively the same thing - "Firing a gun" and "Firing a shell". The smaller bullets fired by launched shotgun are not affected by the player's Damage stat. They are however affected by Bullet Modifiers and items that adjust projectile damage directly such as Cursed Bullets or Gilded Bullets.

entries.englishNotes: Poxcannon is a gun that fires poisonous t-shirts that have significant knockback. Enemies poisoned by this gun leave behind a pool of poison creep upon death. Trivia: Despite the Poxcannon poisoning enemies, the gun is not a member of the POISON gun class. This weapon is a reference to the 'Pax Pox', a blanket term for diseases contracted at conventions, named after the Penny Arcade Expo, or 'PAX' convention. This is confirmed by a tweet released by the official Dodge Roll Games Twitter account on the 27th of April 2016 in which they announced "Enter the Gungeon brought the PoxCannon b...

entries.englishNotes: Directional Pad is a gun that fires bullets in four directions. Upon running out of ammo, the gun is destroyed and spawns a chest of any quality. Entering certain button combinations can cause certain special effects: Notes: Wrath Of The Blam - If the player has Tear Jerker, Tear Jerker is replaced with a crying bullet head that can only fire in cardinal directions but deals double damage. The grappling hook can be used to steal items from shops. The chest spawned may be a Mimic. Trivia: The inputs required to make the special shots are based on famous special attacks from fighting game series: The Fireball is the "Hadouken" attack performed by character Ryu in Street Fighter.

entries.englishNotes: Mourning Star is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a thin red targeting laser that does no damage. Focusing the targeting beam on an enemy will, after a short delay, call down an extremely powerful orbital laser strike at their location. After an orbital laser has been called in, it will persist as long as the trigger is held. Extant orbital lasers will slowly move in the direction of the player's crosshair. Notes: Inactive mimics will not be activated by the tracking beam, and cannot be targeted for a strike. The tracking beam will destroy destructibles, such as the Beholster's RC rockets. The impact damage of the beam is unaffected by passive items.

entries.englishNotes: Triple Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Transitions between different forms based on its current ammo count. When at 70% ammo on the Revolver form (175 ammo if ammo capactiy has not been modified) it will transition to Machine Gun form. When at 10% ammo on the Machine Gun form (50 ammo if ammo capactiy has not been modified) it will transition to Beam Form. Notes: This gun is a reference to the anime Trigun, with its first form's appearance resembling the revolver used by its main character, Vash the Stampede. The line, "this gun is in actuality a very rare plant," is a reference to the Plants in Trigun, interdimensional beings whose bodies produce seemingly endless amounts of luminous...

entries.englishNotes: The Judge is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The last shot of each magazine will fire one of the following special projectiles, chosen at random; A green bullet that deals 4 damage, Poisons (Dur: 2.5s DPS: 3) enemies, and leaves a small puddle of poison goop. A flare that deals 4 damage and sets enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: This gun is a reference to Judge Dredd's gun, the Lawgiver. The pickup quote "Hot Shot" is one of the Lawgiver's settings. The entry for The Judge in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a round-faced figure holding a gun.

entries.englishNotes: Combined Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Reloading alternates between two different firing modes. The first firing mode is a rapid-fire rifle. The second firing mode is a bouncing, piercing energy ball that disintegrates enemies it kills. Notes: The Combined Rifle is a reference to the Overwatch Standard Issue Pulse Rifle, also known as the AR2, from Half-Life 2. The name is a direct reference to the Combine from Half-Life 2. The pickup line, "Halve lives", is also a reference to the name of the game Half-Life.

entries.englishNotes: Balloon Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: The Balloon gun will still pop if the player takes damage to Armor. ETG If the Balloon Gun loses its ammo due to taking damage, it will be rendered invisible, having no sprite until ammo is restored. If the Balloon Gun is dropped while 'popped', it can be very difficult to retrieve due to being completely invisible. It is not, however, impossible to recollect the weapon.

ETG If the 气球枪 loses its ammo due to taking damage, it will be rendered invisible, having no sprite until ammo is restored. If the 气球枪 is dropped while 'popped', it can be very difficult to retrieve due to being completely invisible. It is not, however, impossible to recollect the weapon.

entries.englishNotes: Chance Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Upon firing, adds a chance to fire projectiles from the player's other guns. Shots fired this way from other guns do not consume ammo from that gun. Notes: Maximize Spell - If the player also has Flame Hand, Flame Hand becomes green and creates pools of green fire. Turret Link - If the player also has Portable Turret, Portable Turret will sometimes fire shots from guns in the player's inventory. This item has no effect if the player only has one gun. Trivia: This item has a D20 at its top, which is a die used in RPG games to determine the intensity of what happens to players.

Shots fired this way from other guns do not consume ammo from that gun. Notes: Maximize Spell - If the player also has 火焰之手, 火焰之手 becomes green and creates pools of green fire. Turret Link - If the player also has 便携式炮塔, 便携式炮塔 will sometimes fire shots from guns in the player's inventory. This item has no effect if the player only has one gun. Trivia: This item has a D20 at its top, which is a die used in 火箭推进式榴弹 games to determine the intensity of what happens to players.

entries.englishNotes: Stout Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: At close range, bullets are 50% larger and deal significantly more damage. The damage multiplier at close range is based on the projectiles original range stat, equal to 1.125 + 0.625 * (range - 7) / 15, with a minimum multiplier of 1.125x and a maximum... Any gun with 22 range or higher will have the maximum damage increase of 75%. Notes: Thrown guns will also increase in size and remain large after being picked up. If the player also has Fat Bullets, bullets will become extremely large. Upon firing, they may make contact with a wall and disappear instantly.

If the player also has Fat Bullets, bullets will become extremely large. Upon firing, they may make contact with a wall and disappear instantly.

entries.englishNotes: Bloody 9mm is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Adds an 8% chance per second for a bullet to be replaced by a fast, homing, piercing, and bouncing bullet that flies around the room damaging enemies. Notes: The chance to fire a berserk round is higher for slower weapons. For example, it is nearly 100% for a fully charged BSG. This item can be detrimental with certain items, such as the Black Hole Gun, since it completely replaces the gun’s regular shot. Owning duplicates of this item will not increase its chance of the effect.

Effects: Adds an 8% chance per second for a bullet to be replaced by a fast, homing, piercing, and bouncing bullet that flies around the room damaging enemies. Notes: The chance to fire a berserk round is higher for slower weapons. For example, it is nearly 100% for a fully charged BSG. This item can be detrimental with certain items, such as the Black Hole Gun, since it completely replaces the gun’s regular shot. Owning duplicates of this item will not increase its chance of the effect.

entries.englishNotes: Lament Configurum is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, spawns 3 to 5 enemies. If opened, it will spawn a gun or item. There is a 25% chance that it will damage the player upon spawning a gun or item. Damage starts at one heart and increases by half a heart each time the player is damaged by the item. Notes: Possible enemies spawned include Ashen Bullet Kin, Fallen Bullet Kin, Creeches, Executioners, Bloodbulons, and Poopulons. Lament Configurum is sold for the price of an item in shops even though it is a item. Flynt does not sell it for an increased price.

There is a 25% chance that it will damage the player upon spawning a gun or item. Damage starts at one heart and increases by half a heart each time the player is damaged by the item. Notes: Possible enemies spawned include Ashen Bullet Kin, Fallen Bullet Kin, Creeches, Executioners, Bloodbulons, and Poopulons. 悲伤拼图盒 is sold for the price of an item in shops even though it is a item. Flynt does not sell it for an increased price.

entries.englishNotes: Springheel Boots is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Allows the player to dodge roll again mid-roll, interrupting their initial roll and allowing them to quickly change directions. Notes: This item is a reference to Spring-heeled Jack, an English urban legend from the Victorian era. Its Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the Boots of Springheel Jak from The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion, which are used to survive a long fall as part of The Ultimate Heist quest. This item was originally suggested by a now-deleted account in a suggestion Reddit thread.

entries.englishNotes: Charming Rounds is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets have a 7.5% chance to charm enemies, indicated by being tinted pink. The chance for any given projectile to charm is multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the player's current gun. Beams have a 10% chance per second to charm enemies while shooting them. Notes: A depiction of Charming Rounds can be seen in the opening scroll of the Supply Drop Trailer.
entries.chineseNotes: 魅力弹丸 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets have a 7.5% chance to charm enemies, indicated by being tinted pink. Notes: A depiction of 魅力弹丸 can be seen in the opening scroll of the Supply Drop Trailer.

entries.englishNotes: Zombie Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: 33% chance for missed shots to refund 1 ammo to their source gun. Projectiles are considered to have missed if they have hit zero enemies by the time they are destroyed. This means that bullets that pierce through enemies will not count as missed shots, even if they go on to hit a wall.
entries.chineseNotes: 僵尸子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: 33% chance for missed shots to refund 1 ammo to their source gun. Projectiles are considered to have missed if they have hit zero enemies by the time they are destroyed. This means that bullets that pierce through enemies will not count as missed shots, even if they go on to hit a wall.

entries.englishNotes: Battle Standard is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the damage of most companions and charmed enemies by 80%. Has no effect on Dog or Blank Companion's Ring. Increases the duration of the charm effect. Notes: Having the Clown Mask along with the Drill and/or the Loot Bag will unlock this item. This item appears on the character's back when picked up, making it one of the few passive items that affects the character's appearance. Battle standards, also known as war flags, were carried by numerous land armies throughout history.
entries.chineseNotes: 战旗 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the damage of most companions and charmed enemies by 80%. Has no effect on Dog or Blank Companion's Ring. Increases the duration of the charm effect. Notes: Having the Clown Mask along with the Drill and/or the Loot Bag will unlock this item. This item appears on the character's back when picked up, making it one of the few passive items that affects the character's appearance. Battle standards, also known as war flags, were carried by numerous land armies throughout history.

entries.englishNotes: Remote Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fired bullets curve according to the player's crosshair movement, or by using the directional movement buttons. Effectively provides 100% accuracy to most weapons, which is especially potent for shotguns. This also applies to bullets not directly fired from guns, such as Gunboots, Baby Good Mimic, Super Space Turtle, or Cat Bullet King Throne. Notes: Bullets can be curved around cover, allowing the player to attack from a place of safety. Guns with extremely fast moving projectiles, such as the Flash Ray, are still affected by this item, but their extreme speed makes guiding their bullets effectively impossible.
entries.chineseNotes: 远程子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fired bullets curve according to the player's crosshair movement, or by using the directional movement buttons. Effectively provides 100% accuracy to most weapons, which is especially potent for shotguns. This also applies to bullets not directly fired from guns, such as 长靴, 拟身怪乖宝宝, 超级太空龟, or 猫弹王王座. Notes: Bullets can be curved around cover, allowing the player to attack from a place of safety. Guns with extremely fast moving projectiles, such as the 闪光枪, are still affected by this item, but their extreme speed makes guiding their bullets effectively impossible.

entries.englishNotes: Flak Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Whenever a bullet hits an enemy or wall, or reaches its maximum range, it splits into several smaller, lower-damage bullets that travel outwards within a limited range. These smaller bullets do not adopt any special properties of the original projectile. The amount of flak projectiles created is based on the firerate of the original gun, with slower firing guns creating more flak. Notes: Piercing bullets will not split when hitting enemies, but will split when hitting a wall or reaching their maximum range.

entries.englishNotes: Gilded Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage based on the amount of the player has. Damage can increase up to a maximum of 100% at 500. Any beyond 500 will not increase damage any further. The damage increase is calculated using the formula ([]/500) * (2 - []/500): Notes: With this item, most bullets will turn gold in appearance. If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases the damage based on the amount of the player has.

entries.englishNotes: Magic Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Every bullet fired has a 4%† chance to transmogrify enemies it hits into harmless Chickens. † The chance for any given projectile transmogrify in is multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the player's current gun. Notes: The tagline is a reference to the third Clarke's Law (formulated by Arthur C. Clarke): "Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic." The synergy Chicken Arise is a reference to Aqua Teen Hunger Force season 3 episode 1, entitled "Video Ouija"

entries.englishNotes: Bow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges to shoot arrows. If fired fully charged, its arrows will move faster, deal more damage, and pierce enemies. Notes: Arrowkin wield the Bow. The charge effect is likely a reference to the game "Titan Souls". It could also be a reference to the Light Arrow from The Legend of Zelda games.
entries.chineseNotes: 弓 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges to shoot arrows. If fired fully charged, its arrows will move faster, deal more damage, and pierce enemies. Notes: Arrowkin wield the 弓. The charge effect is likely a reference to the game "Titan Souls". It could also be a reference to the Light Arrow from The Legend of Zelda games.

entries.englishNotes: Relodestone is an active item. Effects: While active, attracts nearby bullets and converts them to ammo. Halves reload times when held. Notes: Relodestar - If the player has any Ammolet, after Relodestone's effect ends, the bullets that were absorbed are shot out of the player dealing damage to enemies. Relodestone prevents damage from bullets while it's active, but does not prevent other damage such as falling, burning, poison and contact damage. Relodestone can refill the Makeshift Cannon right after firing it, making it possible to fire multiple Makeshift Cannon shots in rapid succession. Trivia: Relodestone is the only -rank item unlocked when starting a new game.
entries.chineseNotes: 瑞罗德石 is an active item. Effects: While active, attracts nearby bullets and converts them to ammo. Halves reload times when held. Notes: Relodestar - If the player has any Ammolet, after 瑞罗德石's effect ends, the bullets that were absorbed are shot out of the player dealing damage to enemies. 瑞罗德石 prevents damage from bullets while it's active, but does not prevent other damage such as falling, burning, poison and contact damage. 瑞罗德石 can refill the 临时加农炮 right after firing it, making it possible to fire multiple 临时加农炮 shots in rapid succession. Trivia: 瑞罗德石 is the only -rank item unlocked when starting a new game.

entries.englishNotes: Vorpal Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Every shot has a 1% chance to be replaced by a crit-shot that instantly hits the nearest enemy in the direction the player is aiming, dealing 100 damage. Crit-shots are more common the higher the player's Coolness stat is. Each level of coolness increases the percentage chance of firing a crit-shot by 1. In co-op mode, this effect is based on the combined coolness of both players, not just the one holding the gun. Notes: Because crit-shots instantly hit the nearest enemy in the aimed direction, they do not interact with environmental objects such as barrels, cannot break chests or secret room wall...

entries.englishNotes: Silver Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage to jammed enemies by 225% for a total damage output of 325%. Increases damage to bosses by 25%. Tints player projectiles and beams a silver colour. Notes: This item is a reference to the common folklore trope of silver bullets, often spoken of as the only effective weapon capable of dispatching werewolves, vampires, or other superna... The name of the synergy Hell Singing is in reference to Abraham Van Helsing, a fictional doctor and scientist from Bram Stoker's Dracula.

entries.englishNotes: Boxing Glove is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a limited range punch with a 30% chance to stun enemies for 3 seconds. Despite being a charged gun, the base punch has a charge time of 0 seconds, and can be rapid fired. Killing an enemy with the Boxing Glove adds a star to the gun, shown on the UI above the ammo counter, to a maximum of 3 stars. Holding down the fire button to charge the gun with 3 stars will prepare a high damage super punch, consuming the stars. Notes: This gun is a reference to Mike Tyson's Punch Out!!. It is also a reference to (and example of) the Extendo Boxing Glove, which has appeared broadly in film, television, and literature.

Despite being a charged gun, the base punch has a charge time of 0 seconds, and can be rapid fired. Killing an enemy with the 拳击手套 adds a star to the gun, shown on the UI above the ammo counter, to a maximum of 3 stars. Holding down the fire button to charge the gun with 3 stars will prepare a high damage super punch, consuming the stars. Notes: This gun is a reference to Mike Tyson's Punch Out!!. It is also a reference to (and example of) the Extendo 拳击手套, which has appeared broadly in film, television, and literature.

entries.englishNotes: Glass Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to shoot a powerful piercing bolt of light. This projectile ignores the Boss DPS cap. If the player takes damage while holding the Glass Cannon it will shatter, losing all its ammunition. Notes: While shattered (empty due to taking damage), the Glass Cannon has no sprite, effectively making it invisible. If the Glass Cannon is emptied by using up its ammo reserve instead of taking damage, the gun remains unshattered and can be dropped and retrieved like any other. Unlike the Balloon Gun, it's not possible to drop the weapon while it's shattered. This also makes selling it to the Sell Creep impossible in this state.

entries.englishNotes: Casey is a weapon in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: Jammed bullets and enemies do not deal additional damage when reflected/launched. Firing Casey nudges the player forward a moderate amount. Be mindful near pits to avoid falling. Casey can deflect the High Dragun's rocket attack, but the deflected rocket will not deal damage. Trivia: This weapon is a reference to Ernest Thayer's 1888 poem Casey at the Bat. This weapon is also a reference to the Casey Bat from Earthbound, also named after the poem. The quote "Nothing can happen until you swing the bat." is from FLCL.

entries.englishNotes: Strafe Gun (stylized as STRAFE® Gun) is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires extremely fast moving nails that stick into walls and enemies. Nails that stick into enemies will trigger a cosmetic bleed effect, with the enemy gushing blood onto the ground. Nails will explode after a short time, or all at once when the trigger is released. Notes: Nails from this gun cannot be caught by Gunjurers. Emptying an entire magazine into a boss all at once is inadvisable, as the boss DPS cap will severely limit the damage potential of a large number of nails exploding simultaneousl... This gun is a reference to the game Strafe, specifically to the nail gun upgrade for the machine gun.

entries.englishNotes: The Predator is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Locks onto enemies and fires high speed homing (Rad: 20 Vel: 720) lasers. ETG Upon reloading an empty clip, The Predator grants Stealth, which prevents enemies from noticing the player and allows the player to steal from shops. ETG Highlights hidden Mimics. Notes: The homing laser of The Predator cannot be caught by Gunjurers. This gun is a reference to the film Predator. This gun's description is a reference to a quote from the film, "If it bleeds, we can kill it."

entries.englishNotes: Patriot is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires high-accuracy bullets that can pierce up to 3 times. Rate of fire increases the longer the trigger is held. Notes: This gun is a reference to the weapon of the same name in Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater. The Ammonomicon quote "Gun Eater" is a reference to the title of the game- "Snake Eater". This gun's description is a reference to the convoluted and hard to follow plot of the Metal Gear series, and how people still enjoy it regardless.

This gun's description is a reference to the convoluted and hard to follow plot of the Metal Gear series, and how people still enjoy it regardless.

entries.englishNotes: AC-15 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has two different forms, transforming between them depending on whether or not the player has Armor. Without Armor, this gun fires low-damage purple laser blasts at a high rate of fire. With Armor, this gun fires fast blue blasts with slower rate of fire and high knockback. These blue blasts can pierce up to 3 times. Notes: If all armor is lost while AC-15 is in its armored form, it plays a short breaking animation when returning to its original form. The Robot will always have the armored form due to The Robot having armor instead of hearts.

entries.englishNotes: Windgunner is a gun that is temporarily given to the player when Sprun transforms. It cannot reveal secret rooms. Notes: It cannot be dropped. While Windgunner is held, no other guns can be used or switched to. Trivia: This gun (and the Sprun) are a reference to the Stormlight Archive novels by Brandon Sanderson. Bug: under some circumstances it is possible to have windgunner and be able to swap off it, if you have metronome swapping with this bug in effect will not remove the streak, plea...

Bug: under some circumstances it is possible to have windgunner and be able to swap off it, if you have metronome swapping with this bug in effect will not remove the streak, plea...

entries.englishNotes: Knight's Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires five bullets in a fixed arrow-shaped spread. Upon reloading an empty magazine, the player digs into the ground with the gun, potentially unearthing one of a number of items from the below tables. The Knight's Gun has a 50% chance to unearth an item from the Good Digs Loot Table so long as it has not already unearthed a good dig in the current room. Notes: The digging effect bypasses the item acquirement restrictions of Rainbow Mode. If the player has used Spice, drops have a chance to be replaced with more Spice (even the 1 casing after all good digs have been expended).

entries.englishNotes: Crown of Guns is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires rapidly in all directions simultaneously. ETG Consumes 3 ammo and fires 3 bullets per shot. Bullets slightly home towards enemies. Notes: This gun is likely a reference to the Crown of Guns in Nuclear Throne, a passive companion that increases the amount of gun drops in levels. The name of the Blast Crown synergy is also a reference to a crown in Nuclear Throne. It may also be a reference to the eponymous crown in the book A Crown of Swords, seventh book in the Wheel of Time series.

entries.englishNotes: Bottle is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, stores an extra pickup. Using the Bottle again consumes the pickup. By using it in a room with a Gun Fairy, it's possible to capture it. Notes: When playing as The Robot, Patches and Mendy may give this item to The Robot even if it has not yet been unlocked. When playing as The Robot, the friendly fairy will do nothing and will disappear after a while, due to the fact that he has no heart containers. The item can be used on a health dispenser. If the dispenser holds two half hearts or more, the bottle will store a full heart.

entries.englishNotes: The Fat Line is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires purple particles, which start at the furthest wall in the direction that the gun is aimed, and travel back towards the gun. These projectiles are spread out in the same way as regular bullets, but in reverse. These bullets travel to where the barrel of The Fat Line was when they were fired. Moving with the gun while the particle is in-flight will not update its trajectory. Notes: Does not reveal Secret Rooms. Fixed Bug In early versions of the AG&D Update, this gun could appear in Blessed Mode- despite not being finished or officially released.

entries.englishNotes: The Exotic is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an explosive missile that can pierce up to 10 enemies. While held, grants a small robotic companion that blocks bullets and reveals the contents of nearby chests. If the player has revealed the contents of a chest, the contents of that chest will be permanently locked in as that item. Notes: The Exotic is a reference to the exotic-rarity rocket launcher Gjallarhorn from Destiny, one of the most beloved and memetic exotics in the game. The robot companion is a reference to the Ghosts from Destiny.

While held, grants a small robotic companion that blocks bullets and reveals the contents of nearby chests. Notes: 异域者 is a reference to the exotic-rarity rocket launcher Gjallarhorn from Destiny, one of the most beloved and memetic exotics in the game. The robot companion is a reference to the Ghosts from Destiny.

entries.englishNotes: Full Metal Jacket is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Automatically uses one of the player's Blanks if they are about to take damage, preventing it. This can negate contact damage, which regular blanks do not normally prevent. Grants one piece of Armor when picked up for the first time. Notes: Full Metal Jacket is extremely valuable for collecting Master Rounds. When damaged by harmful Status Effects, Full Metal Jacket will still use a blank, despite the damage not being caused by bullets. This item is a reference to full metal jacket ammunition, where the lead slug is encased in a 'jacket' comprised of a harder alloy to improve the rounds piercing capability.

entries.englishNotes: Glass Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Breaks if the player takes damage. Notes: The Bullet Kiln synergy grants a Glass Guon Stone whenever Elder Blank is used, up to a maximum of 4. This can be circumvented by dropping Glass Guon Stones until the player only has 3 and using Elder Blank, though this may require wasting a charge of Elder Blank outside of combat... The player can have multiple Glass Guon Stones. There is no limit on the maximum number of Glass Guon Stones the player can have at once.
entries.chineseNotes: 玻璃滚石 is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Breaks if the player takes damage. Notes: The 子弹枪 Kiln synergy grants a 玻璃滚石 whenever 旧式空响弹 is used, up to a maximum of 4. This can be circumvented by dropping Glass Guon Stones until the player only has 3 and using 旧式空响弹, though this may require wasting a charge of 旧式空响弹 outside of combat... The player can have multiple Glass Guon Stones. There is no limit on the maximum number of Glass Guon Stones the player can have at once.

entries.englishNotes: Rad Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Passively grants 2 coolness. When reloading the gun, a mark appears on the reload bar, similar to the Cog of Battle. Pressing reload or firing at the right time will increase the gun's damage. The damage dealt by the gun approximately doubles for the first four reloads. Past that, the damage continues to grow infinitely, but at a slower rate. Notes: Refilling the Rad Gun with an Ammo pickup or taking the elevator to the next chamber is considered an automatic reload. Swapping to another weapon for a few seconds will automatically reload the Rad Gun. There is a limit on the maximum reload combo which depends on the reload speed.
entries.chineseNotes: 拉德枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Passively grants 2 coolness. When reloading the gun, a mark appears on the reload bar, similar to the Cog of Battle. Pressing reload or firing at the right time will increase the gun's damage. The damage dealt by the gun approximately doubles for the first four reloads. Past that, the damage continues to grow infinitely, but at a slower rate. Notes: Refilling the 拉德枪 with an Ammo pickup or taking the elevator to the next chamber is considered an automatic reload. Swapping to another weapon for a few seconds will automatically reload the 拉德枪. There is a limit on the maximum reload combo which depends on the reload speed.

entries.englishNotes: Roll Bomb is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling drops a bomb behind the player that explodes after a few moments, dealing 20 damage to all nearby enemies. If this bomb touches an enemy before it explodes, it will deal an additional 5 damage to that enemy on contact. Notes: Dropped bombs do not damage the player, and do not anger Bello. Dropped bombs can reveal secret room entrances by cracking the walls, but will not break the secret wall down completely like other explosions. Possible Bug While sliding over tables, pressing the roll button again will spawn another bomb.
entries.chineseNotes: 滚动炸弹 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling drops a bomb behind the player that explodes after a few moments, dealing 20 damage to all nearby enemies. If this bomb touches an enemy before it explodes, it will deal an additional 5 damage to that enemy on contact. Notes: Dropped bombs do not damage the player, and do not anger Bello. Dropped bombs can reveal secret room entrances by cracking the walls, but will not break the secret wall down completely like other explosions. entries.englishNotes: Helix Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Doubles the player's bullets and causes them to travel through the air in a helical pattern. Each bullet deals 66% of its original damage, for a net 32% increase in damage if both bullets hit their target. Doubles fired beams. Beams will also travel in progressing helical wave patterns. Notes: Explosion damage will not be affected by damage modifiers, including damage reduction. As such, Helix Bullets will massively buff explosive weapons. When used on a gun with non-zero bullet spread, each shots angle variance will be mirrored between the two helix projectiles.
entries.chineseNotes: 螺旋子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Doubles the player's bullets and causes them to travel through the air in a helical pattern. Each bullet deals 66% of its original damage, for a net 32% increase in damage if both bullets hit their target. Doubles fired beams. Beams will also travel in progressing helical wave patterns. Notes: Explosion damage will not be affected by damage modifiers, including damage reduction. As such, 螺旋子弹 will massively buff explosive weapons. When used on a gun with non-zero bullet spread, each shots angle variance will be mirrored between the two helix projectiles.

entries.englishNotes: Chaos Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Slightly randomises the speed of the players bullets. Each shot fired has a chance to be affected by a number of random modifiers from the following list. 10% chance to add 1 additional bounce. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Taste the Painbow" is a reference to the slogan of the Skittles brand of confectionery, "Taste the Rainbow". This item bearing butterfly wings is a reference to the concept of the butterfly effect in chaos theory. It may also be a reference to the song Bullet With Butterfly Wings by the band Smashing Pumpkins.
entries.chineseNotes: 混沌子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Slightly randomises the speed of the players bullets. Each shot fired has a chance to be affected by a number of random modifiers from the following list. 10% chance to add 1 additional bounce. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Taste the Painbow" is a reference to the slogan of the Skittles brand of confectionery, "Taste the Rainbow". This item bearing butterfly wings is a reference to the concept of the butterfly effect in chaos theory. It may also be a reference to the song Bullet With Butterfly Wings by the band Smashing Pumpkins.

entries.englishNotes: Yellow Chamber is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon entering a room, there is a 60% chance that an enemy will become charmed for the duration of the room. Like all charmed enemies, attacks from this enemy can still harm the player. When the room is cleared, the enemy charmed by the Yellow Chamber (provided it is still alive) will be briefly stunned while grey tentacles erupt from the floor and kill it. Notes: Enemies are only charmed in the initial combat wave. Reinforcement enemy waves are unaffected. Enemies encountered when using the Lament Configurum, a Challenge Shrine, or the Drill can be charmed by this item on the first wave of any encounter as normal.
entries.chineseNotes: 黄色膛室 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon entering a room, there is a 60% chance that an enemy will become charmed for the duration of the room. Like all charmed enemies, attacks from this enemy can still harm the player. When the room is cleared, the enemy charmed by the 黄色膛室 (provided it is still alive) will be briefly stunned while grey tentacles erupt from the floor and kill it. Notes: Enemies are only charmed in the initial combat wave. Reinforcement enemy waves are unaffected. Enemies encountered when using the Lament Configurum, a Challenge Shrine, or the Drill can be charmed by this item on the first wave of any encounter as normal.

entries.englishNotes: Cursed Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage by 10% per point of Curse the player has, to a maximum of +100% damage at 10 curse. Increases Curse by 1. Colours the owners projectiles purple and gives them a dark blue smoke trail. This can make the normally invisible projectiles of some guns visible. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Too Spooky" is a reference to the internet meme '2spooky4me'.
entries.chineseNotes: 被诅咒的子弹 is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage by 10% per point of Curse the player has, to a maximum of +100% damage at 10 curse. Increases Curse by 1. Colours the owners projectiles purple and gives them a dark blue smoke trail. This can make the normally invisible projectiles of some guns visible. Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "Too Spooky" is a reference to the internet meme '2spooky4me'.

entries.englishNotes: Chicken Flute is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Spawns a chicken that follows the player, blocks bullets, and deals 2.5 contact damage per frame to enemies it touches. If the chicken takes damage 8 or more times in quick succession, it will summon an army of 20 chickens to attack all enemies in the room. The chickens summoned by this attack each deal 5 damage for a total of 100 damage per chicken swarm. Notes: This item is a reference to Cuccos in the Legend of Zelda series, where if Link attacks a Cucco three times, an army of Cuccos will appear, attacking the player.
entries.chineseNotes: 鸡长笛 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Spawns a chicken that follows the player, blocks bullets, and deals 2.5 contact damage per frame to enemies it touches. If the chicken takes damage 8 or more times in quick succession, it will summon an army of 20 chickens to attack all enemies in the room. The chickens summoned by this attack each deal 5 damage for a total of 100 damage per chicken swarm. Notes: This item is a reference to Cuccos in the Legend of Zelda series, where if Link attacks a Cucco three times, an army of Cuccos will appear, attacking the player.

entries.englishNotes: Chest Teleporter is an active item. Effects: When used on a chest, teleports it to the next chamber. Teleported chests will either appear in the starting room or the room right before the boss room. Teleported chests have a chance to upgrade in quality. Notes: Second Accident - If the player also has Shotbow, its firing pattern is changed to a grid of nine arrows. Telefrag - If the player also has Teleporter Prototype, uses it, and gets teleported to an uncleared room, all enemies in the room will be killed instantly. Mirror chests and Rat chests cannot be teleported.

Teleported chests will either appear in the starting room or the room right before the boss room. Teleported chests have a chance to upgrade in quality. Mirror chests and Rat chests cannot be teleported.

entries.englishNotes: Blasphemy is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, where it functions as the starting weapon of The Bullet. Effects: Fires a piercing rainbow sword projectile when swung. ETG This projectile is only spawned if the player is at full health. The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle. When swung, Blasphemy creates a wide arc that destroys enemy bullets caught within.
entries.chineseNotes: 渎神 is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, where it functions as the starting weapon of The Bullet. Effects: Fires a piercing rainbow sword projectile when swung. ETG This projectile is only spawned if the player is at full health. The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle. When swung, 渎神 creates a wide arc that destroys enemy bullets caught within.

entries.englishNotes: Bug Boots is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. Notes: Use of this item allows the player to spread poison over the entrance to Bullet Hell, despite there visually being no ground to spread it on.
entries.chineseNotes: 臭虫靴子 is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. Notes: Use of this item allows the player to spread poison over the entrance to Bullet Hell, despite there visually being no ground to spread it on.

entries.englishNotes: Robot's Left Hand is a gun that rapidly fires fast moving blue lasers. Notes: In the Robot's past, the Robot starts equipped with this gun. The Player's Character can wield this weapon in their right hand. Trivia: Unused: Look, No Hands - (CustomSynergyType.ROBOT_HANDS) If the player has Robot's Right Hand, the guns are dual wielded. Removed because it contains a starting weapon. Unused: Terminated - (CustomSynergyType.THE_TERMINATOR) If the player has Winchester, it causes an unknown effect. See You Later is a reference to the famous quote "Hasta la vista" from The Terminator.
entries.chineseNotes: Robot's Left Hand is a gun that rapidly fires fast moving blue lasers. Notes: In the Robot's past, the Robot starts equipped with this gun. The Player's Character can wield this weapon in their right hand. Trivia: Unused: Look, No Hands - (CustomSynergyType.ROBOT_HANDS) If the player has Robot's Right Hand, the guns are dual wielded. See You Later is a reference to the famous quote "Hasta la vista" from The Terminator.

entries.englishNotes: Turbo-Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Pressing the reload button while this gun is reloading will increase the damage of its next magazine. The first 5 button presses each add an additional 20% damage, while any subsequent presses add 10%. Capped at an additional 300% damage after 25 button presses. Notes: Requiring a click rate of 25 clicks per second, the player will likely be unable to achieve full damage every time. This can be worked around on PC by temporarily binding the player's reload keys to ScrollUp and ScrollDown on the scroll wheel. Autoclickers make the Turbo-Gun an excellent choice against bosses.

The first 5 button presses each add an additional 20% damage, while any subsequent presses add 10%. Capped at an additional 300% damage after 25 button presses. Notes: Requiring a click rate of 25 clicks per second, the player will likely be unable to achieve full damage every time. entries.englishNotes: Sprun is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a friendly green sprite to follow the player. This sprite can block enemy bullets. When picked up, Sprun will select a random trigger from a list of possible events. The full list of possible triggers for Sprun is as follows; Notes: Sprun can trigger even while in a Blessed Run. If Sprun's trigger is falling into a pit, entering the Oubliette may trigger Sprun until the player falls into a pit again. Even if the player has fire immunity, Sprun can activate when standing inside of it.
entries.chineseNotes: 弹簧支撑 is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a friendly green sprite to follow the player. This sprite can block enemy bullets. When picked up, 弹簧支撑 will select a random trigger from a list of possible events. The full list of possible triggers for 弹簧支撑 is as follows; Notes: 弹簧支撑 can trigger even while in a Blessed Run. entries.englishNotes: Blank Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Bullets have a chance of triggering a short-range blank when they hit obstacles or enemies. Doubles the damage of beam style Guns. Increases curse by 1.5. Notes: Elder Blank Bullets - If the player also has Elder Blank, the blank effects reflect all projectiles instead of deleting them. Blanks triggered by Blank Bullets are affected by Ammolets and causes the short ranged blanks to function strangely. Blank Bullets combined with Gold Ammolet can clear rooms without entering them. Trivia: The tagline is a reference to the well-known adage "The best defense is a good offense".
entries.chineseNotes: 空响弹子弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Bullets have a chance of triggering a short-range blank when they hit obstacles or enemies. Doubles the damage of beam style Guns. Increases curse by 1.5. Notes: Elder 空响弹子弹 - If the player also has 旧式空响弹, the blank effects reflect all projectiles instead of deleting them. Blanks triggered by 空响弹子弹 are affected by Ammolets and causes the short ranged blanks to function strangely. 空响弹子弹 combined with 金手镯 can clear rooms without entering them. Trivia: The tagline is a reference to the well-known adage "The best defense is a good offense".

entries.englishNotes: Junk is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. It often appears after destroying chests. Effects: Junk has no inherent effect, however it does interact with some other systems and items. When playing as The Robot, each piece of Junk increases damage by a permanent additive 5%. The damage increase persists no matter what happens to the Junk. If the player has Ser Junkan, collecting Junk levels him up, making him more powerful. Notes: Junk has a chest destruction loot weight of 0.45. If the player is in possession of Ser Junkan, the chest destruction loot weight of junk is multiplied by 1.5. If the player is in possession of at least one key, the chest destruction loot weight of junk is multiplied by 3.

entries.englishNotes: Void Core Cannon is a gun that fires bursts of four large energy balls, which home in on enemies and explode upon impact. Notes: This gun uses the same sprite as the unused Void Core Rocket Launcher. This gun is a reference to the Plasma Projector carried by the Assault T-Synth from Void 1.1. Both Glass Cannon and Void Core Cannon have "Cannon" in their name, yet do not trigger the The Return synergy.

entries.englishNotes: Moonlight Tiara is an unused gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Moonlight Tiara was re-used as an alternate form of the Crown of Guns with the Alas, Sniperion synergy.
entries.chineseNotes: Moonlight Tiara is an unused gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Moonlight Tiara was re-used as an alternate form of the Crown of Guns with the Alas, Sniperion synergy.

entries.englishNotes: Life Orb is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: The reload attack is based on enemies killed with gunfire while holding the Life Orb, not necessarily kills with the Life Orb. It is possible to break the damage cap with the Life Orb, but only against the Lich. This item is a reference to an item from the Pokémon series of the same name and similar appearance.
entries.chineseNotes: 生命球 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: The reload attack is based on enemies killed with gunfire while holding the 生命球, not necessarily kills with the 生命球. It is possible to break the damage cap with the 生命球, but only against the Lich. This item is a reference to an item from the Pokémon series of the same name and similar appearance.

entries.englishNotes: Teapot is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires heart-shaped clouds of steam. The final shot of every magazine is pink, and deals additional damage. In co-op mode, if the final shot hits the other co-op player it will heal them for half a heart. Healing another player consumes an additional 24 ammo from the Teapot. Notes: The AoE effect only lasts as long as the weapon's reload time. This also applies to Cog of Battle, in which case the effect is also canceled early by the active reload, making it often favorable to not use the active reload to prolong the AoE...

In co-op mode, if the final shot hits the other co-op player it will heal them for half a heart. Healing another player consumes an additional 24 ammo from the 茶壶. Notes: The AoE effect only lasts as long as the weapon's reload time. This also applies to Cog of Battle, in which case the effect is also canceled early by the active reload, making it often favorable to not use the active reload to prolong the AoE...

entries.englishNotes: Mr. Accretion Jr. is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Mr. Accretion Jr. fires planets that resemble the 8 planets of the solar system. Notes: Firing or reloading Mr. Accretion Jr. releases a cloud of gas with a space texture. This effect is purely visual. If the player attempts to reload when the gun is already full, it will still release this gas. This gas is very similar to that released by the time rip that unlocks the Paradox.
entries.chineseNotes: 小冲积先生 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: 小冲积先生 fires planets that resemble the 8 planets of the solar system. Notes: Firing or reloading 小冲积先生 releases a cloud of gas with a space texture. This effect is purely visual. If the player attempts to reload when the gun is already full, it will still release this gas. This gas is very similar to that released by the time rip that unlocks the Paradox.

entries.englishNotes: Stone Dome is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing (Rad: 25 Vel: 360) projectiles. While held, any non-boss enemies within 90° of the angle the player is aiming will become afraid, fleeing the player. This effect works over any distance, however enemies will only start to flee the player after coming within a 6 tile range. Notes: The fear status effect can compel enemies to walk backwards into hazards such as dangerous goop or pits. Bug Switching to another gun causes currently afraid enemies to be permanently afraid.
entries.chineseNotes: 石质圆顶 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing (Rad: 25 Vel: 360) projectiles. While held, any non-boss enemies within 90° of the angle the player is aiming will become afraid, fleeing the player. This effect works over any distance, however enemies will only start to flee the player after coming within a 6 tile range. Notes: The fear status effect can compel enemies to walk backwards into hazards such as dangerous goop or pits. Bug Switching to another gun causes currently afraid enemies to be permanently afraid.

entries.englishNotes: Bubble Blaster is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires slow moving bubble projectiles. These bubbles are off various sizes, but all deal the same amount of damage. Bubbles create a pool of water when popping on impacting a wall, object, or enemy. This allows them to extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace. Only 10 bubbles can be in the air at once. Firing any more bubbles will cause the oldest bubble to instantly pop. Notes: The Bubble Blaster appears to be a Gunzookie. Like the Gunzookie, the Bubble Blaster is a reference to Bub from the Bubble Bobble franchise, a green lizard that creates bubbles.
entries.chineseNotes: 泡泡喷射枪 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires slow moving bubble projectiles. These bubbles are off various sizes, but all deal the same amount of damage. Bubbles create a pool of water when popping on impacting a wall, object, or enemy. This allows them to extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace. Only 10 bubbles can be in the air at once. Firing any more bubbles will cause the oldest bubble to instantly pop. Notes: The 泡泡喷射枪 appears to be a Gunzookie. Like the Gunzookie, the 泡泡喷射枪 is a reference to Bub from the Bubble Bobble franchise, a green lizard that creates bubbles.

entries.englishNotes: Big Shotgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: ETG The maximum ammo of the Big Shotgun was increased from 50 to 70 in the Farewell to Arms update. Despite being a shotgun, the Big Shotgun is not in the SHOTGUN gun class. As a consequence, the Big Shotgun is not affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy.
entries.chineseNotes: 大型霰弹枪 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: ETG The maximum ammo of the 大型霰弹枪 was increased from 50 to 70 in the Farewell to Arms update. Despite being a 常规霰弹枪, the 大型霰弹枪 is not in the SHOTGUN gun class. As a consequence, the 大型霰弹枪 is not affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy.

entries.englishNotes: GuNNER is a gun. It fires golden bullets, which make a red skull effect when hitting an enemy. Notes: Heads Up - If the player also has Stone Dome, Stone Dome will shoot skulls instead. Whale of a Time - If the player also has Backpack, there's a chance a skull will spawn when getting hit to recover lost health, even if the player has another weapon equipped whil... Picking up the skull while playing as The Robot will give you 1 armor. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the game GoNNER. The Whale of a Time Synergy is a reference to Sally, the helpful whale at the end of each of GoNNER's stages.

entries.englishNotes: Unfinished Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing projectiles. Notes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class. As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies. The Blacksmith can be seen working on an Unfinished Gun.
entries.chineseNotes: 枪坯 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing projectiles. Notes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the 枪坯 is in the FIRE gun class. As a consequence, having the 枪坯 in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies. The Blacksmith can be seen working on an 枪坯.

entries.englishNotes: Slinger is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. It is the sole starting weapon of The Gunslinger. Effects: When reloading an empty magazine, the gun itself is thrown forwards. If this tossed gun hits an enemy, it has a chance to Stun them. Possible Bug After dodge rolling, the firerate of the Slinger is temporarily boosted. Notes: The Slinger has zero bullet spread and its accuracy cannot be decreased, making it one of the most precise guns in the game. The Slinger is the fastest-firing of all the starter weapons. Uniquely, Slinger has two reload animations. If reloaded with an empty clip, the Gunslinger will throw it and get a new one from his belt.

entries.englishNotes: Loot Bag is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Doubles drops from the following sources; Enemy & Boss Drops. Selling items to the Sell Creep. Notes: ETG Dropped that falls into a pit will teleport back to the player's current location, making it easier to pick back up. XTG Dropped that falls into an elevator shaft cannot be recovered.. dropped near a wall is easier to pick up.

entries.englishNotes: Clown Mask is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Visually changes the players appearance to Dallas from the Payday franchise. Summons one of the following clown-masked companions; Increases the likelihood of finding the Loot Bag or Drill. Notes: Despite being a companion item, Clown Mask cannot be obtained from the Companion Shrine. The Clown Mask and its companions are a reference to PAYDAY 2, and were added in a cross-promotional event. In PAYDAY 2, this particular mask belongs to PAYDAY gang mastermind Dallas.

entries.englishNotes: Mutation is a gun that fires a slightly homing continuous beam with heavy recoil, leaving a trail of red liquid. Mutation also turns enemies red, though this is a purely cosmetic effect. Notes: The synergy between the Mutation and Bionic Leg/Shock Rounds may be a reference to Tetsuo from the 1982 manga series Akira. "Didn't Need Two Anyway" refers to the look of the weapon in-game, which envelops the player's whole arm. This visual effect is also shared with Robot's Right Hand, Robot's Left Hand, Heroine, Flame Hand and Megahand.
entries.chineseNotes: 变异手臂 is a gun that fires a slightly homing continuous beam with heavy recoil, leaving a trail of red liquid. 变异手臂 also turns enemies red, though this is a purely cosmetic effect. Notes: The synergy between the 变异手臂 and 仿生腿/电击炮 may be a reference to Tetsuo from the 1982 manga series Akira. entries.englishNotes: Rubenstein's Monster is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It is created solely as the result of combining the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype and RUBE-ADYNE MK.II with the Rubenstein's Monster synergy. Effects: Rapidly fires pink laser blasts that can bounce once. Notes: This gun is not needed for completion. The Finished Gun can be unlocked without having this gun's Ammonomicon entry filled. Despite its considerable power, Rubenstein's Monster is in the SHITTY gun class. Since Rubenstein's Monster is excluded from the normal loot pool, this has no effect on the player's likelihood to obtain the gun, but it will still reduce the chance of finding o...

entries.englishNotes: Wood Beam is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: While the trigger is held, this gun will extend in the form of a stiff beam until reaching the end of its range. Swinging the beam into enemies deals considerably more damage and knockback than direct hits. The beam deals 1 damage per second when used to hit directly like a normal beam weapon. Notes: The Wood Beam deals no damage to chests. For this reason, it can be an excellent way to check for Mimics. Wood Beam's damage multiplier from swing speed applies after Unity's flat damage bonus.

entries.englishNotes: AK-47 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the AK-47 is called the AP-13. Notes: The AK-47 is wielded by some Bullet KinETG, TankersETG, and HollowpointsXTG. ETG If the player feeds the AK-47 and either the Flame Hand or Megahand to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Machine Fist. The description of the AK-47 is a reference to the film Jackie Brown.

ETG If the player feeds the AK-47 and either the Flame Hand or Megahand to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Machine Fist. The description of the AK-47 is a reference to the film Jackie Brown.

entries.englishNotes: Heroine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It has 3 levels of damage depending on how long the gun has been charged. Notes: The Heroine is a reference to the Arm Cannon wielded by Samus Aran, the heavily armored bounty hunter and protagonist in the Metroid series. Its in-game model and its alternative modes match its appearance in Metroid Prime. The Hyper Beam synergy is a reference to a weapon with the same name from Super Metroid. The beam is obtained after a baby Metroid sacrifices itself to save Samus.

entries.englishNotes: Casey is a weapon in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: Jammed bullets and enemies do not deal additional damage when reflected/launched. Firing Casey nudges the player forward a moderate amount. Be mindful near pits to avoid falling. Casey can deflect the High Dragun's rocket attack, but the deflected rocket will not deal damage. Trivia: This weapon is a reference to Ernest Thayer's 1888 poem Casey at the Bat. This weapon is also a reference to the Casey Bat from Earthbound, also named after the poem. The quote "Nothing can happen until you swing the bat." is from FLCL.

The quote "Nothing can happen until you swing the bat." is from FLCL.

entries.englishNotes: Megahand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires small yellow pellets that deal 6 damage. Charges up to fire a powerful blue energy blast that deals 45 damage and knocks enemies back. Notes: This gun is a reference to Mega Man's arm cannon, called the Mega Buster. The tooltip references how in Mega Man through Mega Man 3, the Mega Buster was represented in menus by the Symbol P. The colors the Megahand flashes while charging most closely resemble those in Mega Man 4, the game in which the ability to charge the Buster was first introduced.

entries.chineseNotes: 空中射手 是《挺进地牢》中的一把枪械。效果：发射 6 点伤害的小型黄色子弹，并可蓄力发射造成 45 点伤害且会击退敌人的强力蓝色能量弹。注释：这把枪致敬《洛克人》的手臂炮 洛克手炮。

entries.englishNotes: Drill is an active item. Before it is unlocked, it has a 5% chance to replace an item in the shop or a 100% chance if the Biggest Wallet achievement has been unlocked, and costs 9999. Effects: If used on a locked chest, teleports the player to a large version of the chest room, where they must survive one to five waves of enemies. If used on a locked door or trapdoor, unlocks the object for free. Notes: Picking up Drill increases the chance of finding the other Payday 2 items, Loot Bag and Clown Mask. The amount of enemies and waves is determined by the chest quality, usually having less enemies and waves when used on low-quality chests, and up to 5 waves for S quality chests.

entries.englishNotes: Elimentaler is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires chunks of cheese that encheese enemies, encasing them in cheese and freezing them. Has a 50% chance to fire a cheese ball that deals 5.5 damage, a 33.33% chance to fire a cheese wedge that deals 8 damage, and a 16.67% chance to fire a whole cheese wheel that dea... Notes: Elimentaler will not reveal secret room walls, nor can be fed to Munchers. Enemies can be encheesed while in invulnerable states (such as Shelletons while turning into a skull), forcing the player to wait until the effect wears off. The pool of cheese left behind by enemies will put out the fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord.

entries.englishNotes: Platinum Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Increases damage and fire rate with every bullet that hits an enemy. Weapon fire rate doubles every 250 normalized seconds of firing (2500 shots with a ten-shots-per-second weapon), to a maximum of triple firing speed after 375 seconds of shooting. Weapon damage dealt doubles every 500 normalized seconds of firing (5000 shots with a ten-shots-per-second weapon), to a maximum of triple damage after 750 seconds of shooting. Notes: The damage and fire rate bonuses do not update until the player switches weapons or picks up/drops an item or gun, making it possible for the bonuses to never apply.

Weapon fire rate doubles every 250 normalized seconds of firing (2500 shots with a ten-shots-per-second weapon), to a maximum of triple firing speed after 375 seconds of shooting. Weapon damage dealt doubles every 500 normalized seconds of firing (5000 shots with a ten-shots-per-second weapon), to a maximum of triple damage after 750 seconds of shooting. entries.englishNotes: Bumbullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While holding down the fire button, the player's current gun will launch a homing (Rad: 10 Vel: 150) bee projectile every second. The initial 3 damage impact of the bee is affected by the players Damage stat, but the lingering swarm damage is not. This extra bee projectile does not consume ammo. Notes: Because bee spawning is based on an independent cooldown while the fire key is held. This includes infinite ammo weapons such as Casey. Bees spawned this way reveal secret walls, and do not anger the Shopkeeper.

The initial 3 damage impact of the bee is affected by the players Damage stat, but the lingering swarm damage is not. This extra bee projectile does not consume ammo. Bees spawned this way reveal secret walls, and do not anger the Shopkeeper.

entries.englishNotes: Holey Grail is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon taking damage, triggers a full screen Blank effect and refills 50% of the ammo of all the player's guns, rounded up. Increases Curse by 1. Notes: The Blank effect of this item can open Secret Rooms. The item's chest tier might be a reference to Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade, where the true Holy Grail is a simple ceramic cup instead of a golden chalice. [citation needed] Dodge Roll developers said in the past that this item may be related to the lore of a later expansion, presumably A Farewell to Arms.

entries.englishNotes: Turkey is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Recovers 1 ammo after landing three sequential shots. For guns that shoot multiple projectiles, landing multiple projectiles from the same volley still only counts as landing one shot. Piercing projectiles that hit multiple enemies will still only count as landing one shot. Notes: The item's icon resembles a drawing of a turkey, created by tracing the outline of a right hand. This item and it's Ammonomicon description are a reference to scoring three strikes in a row in bowling, referred to as a 'turkey'.
entries.chineseNotes: 火鸡球 is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Recovers 1 ammo after landing three sequential shots. For guns that shoot multiple projectiles, landing multiple projectiles from the same volley still only counts as landing one shot. Piercing projectiles that hit multiple enemies will still only count as landing one shot. Notes: The item's icon resembles a drawing of a turkey, created by tracing the outline of a right hand. This item and it's Ammonomicon description are a reference to scoring three strikes in a row in bowling, referred to as a 'turkey'.

entries.englishNotes: Table Tech Shotgun is a table tech passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Flipping a table releases a shotgun blast of 10 homing bullets in the direction the table was flipped. These bullets deal 6 damage each, with a combined damage of 60 per table flip if all bullets find a target. These bullets can also bounce up to 4 times, and are affected by the players bullet modifiers. Notes: There is a small chance for the bullets to overlap with the table when flipped, destroying them instantly and giving the illusion that nothing was fired. The chance greatly increases with items that increase bullet size, like Fat Bullets and Stout Bullets.

These bullets deal 6 damage each, with a combined damage of 60 per table flip if all bullets find a target. entries.englishNotes: Crisis Stone is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants invulnerability while reloading an empty magazine. A voice clip of Doseone saying "Reload" plays every time a gun's magazine is emptied. Notes: This item is a reference to the Namco light gun arcade shooter series Time Crisis. A voice alerting the player that their magazine is empty is a reference to Time Crisis'. The crosshair symbol on the stone is based on the logo of the Time Crisis series.
entries.chineseNotes: 危机石 is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants invulnerability while reloading an empty magazine. A voice clip of Doseone saying "Reload" plays every time a gun's magazine is emptied. Notes: This item is a reference to the Namco light gun arcade shooter series Time Crisis. A voice alerting the player that their magazine is empty is a reference to Time Crisis'. The crosshair symbol on the stone is based on the logo of the Time Crisis series.

entries.englishNotes: Snowballets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets increase in size and damage as they travel. Bullets gain 2.5% additional damage for every tile they travel. Bullets grow by 10% of their original size for every tile they travel. Notes: 150% Damage is reached with 20 tiles; Max damage is reached at 60 tiles or greater. The item's name and primary function is a reference to the popular trope of snowballs increasing in size, often to huge snow boulders, when rolling down a hill. The "sole scarf in the Gungeon" mentioned to in the description might refer to the Bloodied Scarf, as it is the only item called a scarf in the game.
entries.chineseNotes: 雪球弹 is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets increase in size and damage as they travel. Bullets gain 2.5% additional damage for every tile they travel. Bullets grow by 10% of their original size for every tile they travel. Notes: 150% Damage is reached with 20 tiles; Max damage is reached at 60 tiles or greater. The item's name and primary function is a reference to the popular trope of snowballs increasing in size, often to huge snow boulders, when rolling down a hill. The "sole scarf in the Gungeon" mentioned to in the description might refer to the Bloodied Scarf, as it is the only item called a scarf in the game.

entries.englishNotes: Weird Egg is a single-use active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Using the egg while having empty hearts will restore the player to full health. Dropping the egg and shooting it will break the egg, either spawning a random item or gun, or spawning a harmless slug-like yolk creature that follows the player around until they... Going through 2 consecutive floors will cause the egg to start hopping slightly and drop a or tier item or gun when broken. Notes: Pits in the Forge don't count as sources of fire and the egg will be lost upon dropping it in. Dropping the egg on green fire will break the egg and no serpent will appear.

entries.englishNotes: Devolver Rounds is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets have a chance to devolve enemies into Arrowkin. Gives beam style Guns a small chance every second while firing to gain double damage for one second, creating random seconds of double damage. Notes: The devolution effect can trigger on some enemies that are normally invulnerable, such as the Gunreaper or Lead Cube, turning them into vulnerable Arrowkin. The name and appearance of Devolver Rounds are in reference to Devolver Digital, the publishers of Enter the Gungeon.

Gives beam style Guns a small chance every second while firing to gain double damage for one second, creating random seconds of double damage. entries.englishNotes: Vorpal Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Whenever the player fires a gun, occasionally fires a special high-damage bullet, similar to the critical shot fired by the Vorpal Gun. This crit shot deals 100 damage, and is affected by the player's Damage stat. Critical hits do not travel like normal bullets, but instead instantly strike the closest enemy in the cursor's direction regardless of intervening obstacles. Notes: Snicker-snack - If the player has Excaliber, it fires a spread of 3 homing shots that deal 20% less damage.

This crit shot deals 100 damage, and is affected by the player's Damage stat. entries.englishNotes: Gold Junk is a passive item. It can appear after destroying chests. Effects: Grants 500 upon pickup. Notes: Gold Junk has a chest destruction loot weight of 0.005. If the player has Ser Junkan, the weight of Gold Junk in the chest destruction loot table is multiplied by 3, making it more likely to appear. Although a high-quality chest must be destroyed to unlock Gold Junk, it can drop after destroying any chest. Trivia: Unused: Precious Metals - (CustomSynergyType.PLATINUM_AND_GOLD) If the player had Platinum Bullets, killing an enemy would spawn 1-6 extra in addition to the enemy's other loot. The tagline is a reference to the proverb "One man's trash is another man's treasure".

If the player has Ser Junkan, the weight of 金垃圾 in the chest destruction loot table is multiplied by 3, making it more likely to appear. Although a high-quality chest must be destroyed to unlock 金垃圾, it can drop after destroying any chest. The tagline is a reference to the proverb "One man's trash is another man's treasure".

entries.englishNotes: Daruma is an active item. Effects: Can only be used shortly after rolling through a bullet, and activates a blank effect. Notes: Like other active items, Daruma needs to recharge by damaging enemies; it cannot be used immediately after every dodged bullet. If the player has Whiter Guon Stone, Daruma's recharge is halved. However, Daruma itself is not part of the synergy. The blank effect can open secret rooms. Trivia: This item is a reference to a Daruma doll, a round, hollow Japanese doll modeled after Bodhidharma, the founder of the Zen tradition of Buddhism. Each time Daruma is used, its appearance changes.
entries.chineseNotes: 达摩 is an active item. Effects: Can only be used shortly after rolling through a bullet, and activates a blank effect. Notes: Like other active items, 达摩 needs to recharge by damaging enemies; it cannot be used immediately after every dodged bullet. If the player has Whiter Guon Stone, 达摩's recharge is halved. However, 达摩 itself is not part of the synergy. The blank effect can open secret rooms. Trivia: This item is a reference to a 达摩 doll, a round, hollow Japanese doll modeled after Bodhidharma, the founder of the Zen tradition of Buddhism. Each time 达摩 is used, its appearance changes.

entries.englishNotes: Portable Table Device is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Once charged, this item can be used 3 times before needing to charge again. Spawns a small table, which can be flipped to provide cover. The table will be destroyed after taking enough damage. Notes: During the second boss phase of High Dragun, flipping a table clears out nearby bullets immensely. Bug The Ammoconda may become stuck on tables spawned by this item. Comparably to their size, the tables this item spawns are weaker than typical tables.

entries.englishNotes: Turtle Problem is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon clearing a room, spawns a turtle familiar. Turtles can block bullets and deal contact damage to enemies. They will die if they take a certain amount of damage. Notes: The Ammonomicon entry for this item existed unused in the game since the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, but the item was only added properly in A Farewell to Arms. This item may be a reference to the 'Turtles all the way down' method of expressing Infinite Regress. Bug Every time a turtle dies, the error [Error : Unity Log] An enemy who does not have a parent room is dying... this could be a problem. is thrown to the Unity Log.

entries.englishNotes: Chamber Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has 10 different forms, one for each Chamber of The Gungeon that can be accessed in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: All modes share the same ammo pool. Firing a shot in one mode reduces the ammo of the other modes by a proportional amount. The Chamber Gun is one of the three weapons in the NONE gun class that can be obtained in normal play, the others being the Slinger and the Kruller Glaive. If the player has Chance Bullets and does not switch to the Chamber Gun from a floor that does not have a Master Round (such as the Oubliette, Abbey of the True Gun, Resourceful R...

entries.englishNotes: Lower Case r is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a burst of 6 letter-shaped projectiles that spell out the word BULLET. While firing, the gun will voice out the word "bullet". Notes: The Lower Case r is voiced by Doseone, the composer of Enter the Gungeons soundtrack. This gun, while obviously a surface level reference to the basic shape of the letter r, may also be a reference to early computer adventure games. The reload animation of the Lower Case r consists of a text cursor deleting the letter with a backspace, and typing an entirely new r.

entries.englishNotes: Winchester is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. It is similar to the Regular Shotgun, and has a higher fire rate, lower reload time, and less spread, but less max ammo. Notes: The Winchester is wielded by Blue Shotgun Kin. The quote "Better Than A Box Of Roses" is a reference to a scene in the movie Terminator 2: Judgement Day, where the Terminator pulls a Winchester out of a box full of roses. When reloading the Winchester, the player will spin the weapon backwards around the trigger hand. This is in imitation of what the Terminator does in the movie.
